---
title: "HB_dynatop3"
format: html
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
Clean run through with snow

Make a model from start to finish. 

Use base dynaGIS to create model, then add snow parameters.
```{r setup}
# Load packages
library(pacman)
p_load(terra, xts, whitebox, tidyverse,
       lubridate,
       dynatopGIS,
       sf, # working with st and sf objects
       tidyterra, # do tidy ops on terra objects
       sfnetworks, # create start and end nodes for channel network
       lwgeom,
       lhs, DEoptim, # maybe obsolete since I am using GLUE
       parallel,
       readr
       )


# Load modified version of dynatop, which includes snow routine
devtools::load_all(file.path("/Users/johnmorgan/Documents/HBTopModel/revised_package/dynatop")) ## attach the revised package

crs1 <- "PROJCRS[\"NAD83 / UTM zone 19N\",\n    BASEGEOGCRS[\"NAD83\",\n        DATUM[\"North American Datum 1983\",\n            ELLIPSOID[\"GRS 1980\",6378137,298.257222101,\n                LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]]],\n        PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,\n            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433]],\n        ID[\"EPSG\",4269]],\n    CONVERSION[\"UTM zone 19N\",\n        METHOD[\"Transverse Mercator\",\n            ID[\"EPSG\",9807]],\n        PARAMETER[\"Latitude of natural origin\",0,\n            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8801]],\n        PARAMETER[\"Longitude of natural origin\",-69,\n            ANGLEUNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8802]],\n        PARAMETER[\"Scale factor at natural origin\",0.9996,\n            SCALEUNIT[\"unity\",1],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8805]],\n        PARAMETER[\"False easting\",500000,\n            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8806]],\n        PARAMETER[\"False northing\",0,\n            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1],\n            ID[\"EPSG\",8807]]],\n    CS[Cartesian,2],\n        AXIS[\"(E)\",east,\n            ORDER[1],\n            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]],\n        AXIS[\"(N)\",north,\n            ORDER[2],\n            LENGTHUNIT[\"metre\",1]],\n    USAGE[\n        SCOPE[\"Engineering survey, topographic mapping.\"],\n        AREA[\"North America - between 72°W and 66°W - onshore and offshore. Canada - Labrador; New Brunswick; Nova Scotia; Nunavut; Quebec. Puerto Rico. United States (USA) - Connecticut; Maine; Massachusetts; New Hampshire; New York (Long Island); Rhode Island; Vermont.\"],\n        BBOX[14.92,-72,84,-66]],\n    ID[\"EPSG\",26919]]"
```

# GIS PREP
```{r GIS files}
dem_path <- file.path(".", "hb_data", "unprocessed",
                      "1m hydro enforced dem","hydem1mlpns.tif")
dem <- rast(dem_path) # load the dem as a raster layer
plot(dem)

# crop DEM to a rough rectangle
ybounds <- c(4870350,4871350)
xbounds <- c(281350, 282150)
crop1 <- crop(dem, ext(c(xbounds, ybounds)))
plot(crop1)
crop_dem_path <- file.path(".", "hb_data", "unprocessed",
                      "1m hydro enforced dem","hydem1mlpns_cropped.tif")
writeRaster(crop1, crop_dem_path, overwrite = TRUE)
####delineate watershed and keep watershed boundary

w3_breached <- "./hb_data/unprocessed/whitebox_outputs/1mdem_breach.tif"
wbt_breach_depressions_least_cost(
  dem = crop_dem_path,
  output = w3_breached,
  dist = 1,
  fill = TRUE)

w3_filled <- "./hb_data/unprocessed/whitebox_outputs/1mdem_fill.tif"
wbt_fill_depressions_wang_and_liu(
  dem = w3_breached,
  output = w3_filled
)
#calculate flow accumulation and direction
w3_flowacc <- "./hb_data/unprocessed/whitebox_outputs/1mdem_w3_flowacc.tif"
wbt_d8_flow_accumulation(input = w3_filled,
                         output = w3_flowacc)
w3_d8pt <- "./hb_data/unprocessed/whitebox_outputs/1mdem_w3_d8pt.tif"
wbt_d8_pointer(dem = w3_filled,
               output = w3_d8pt)

#delineate streams
w3_streams <- "./hb_data/unprocessed/whitebox_outputs/w3_streams.tif"
wbt_extract_streams(flow_accum = w3_flowacc,
                    output = w3_streams,
                    threshold = 8000)
plot(rast(w3_streams))

# convert streams to shapefile
w3_streams_vect <- "./hb_data/unprocessed/whitebox_outputs/w3_streams_vect.shp"
wbt_raster_streams_to_vector(streams = w3_streams,
                             d8_pntr = w3_d8pt,
                             output = w3_streams_vect)
plot(vect(w3_streams_vect))

# get pour point ready
pour_point_path <- file.path(".", "hb_data",
                             "unprocessed",
                             "1m hydro enforced dem", "w3_pour_snap.shp")
plot(vect(pour_point_path), add = TRUE)

w3_pour_snap <- "./hb_data/unprocessed/whitebox_outputs/w3_pour_snap.shp"
wbt_jenson_snap_pour_points(pour_pts = pour_point_path,
                            streams = w3_streams,
                            output = w3_pour_snap,
                            snap_dist = 10)
w3_pour_snap_read <- vect(w3_pour_snap)
plot(rast(w3_streams), 
     xlim = c(281400, 282000),
     ylim = c(4870400, 4870800))
points(w3_pour_snap_read, pch = 1)


w3_shed <- "./hb_data/unprocessed/whitebox_outputs/w3_shed.tif"
wbt_watershed(d8_pntr = w3_d8pt,
              pour_pts = w3_pour_snap,
              output = w3_shed)

plot(rast(w3_shed))
#convert raster of watershed area to vector for final mapping
w3_outline <- as.polygons(rast(w3_shed), extent=FALSE)
plot(w3_outline)

plot(w3_outline)

channel <- vect(w3_streams_vect, crs = crs1) %>% 
  crop(w3_outline)


# crop and mask DEM by watershed outline
dem_crop_mask <- rast(w3_filled) %>% 
  crop(w3_outline) %>% 
  mask(w3_outline)

#create watershed area, just use area of DEM
outline <- (dem_crop_mask * 0) + 1
# extend both rasters by 1 pixel
outline_extended <- extend(outline,1)
dem_final <- extend(dem_crop_mask,1)


writeRaster(outline_extended,file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed","w3.tif"),overwrite=TRUE)
writeRaster(dem_final,file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed","dem.tif"),overwrite=TRUE)
```
```{r build channel}
# =============================================================================
# build_dynatop_channel.R  (v3 — definitive)
#
# ROOT CAUSE (discovered from HRU 39 endNode=30, sf_flow_direction=47):
#   convert_channel sets each channel HRU's id    = startNode value
#                                    sf_flow_dir  = endNode value
#   It never uses the 'name' column for ordering.
#
# Therefore the startNode/endNode values in the SpatVector passed to
# convert_channel MUST themselves be topologically ordered integers:
#   outlet segment startNode = 1 (lowest)
#   every upstream segment startNode > its downstream segment's startNode
#
# All previous scripts sorted rows and assigned 'name' = 1..N but left the
# original sfnetwork-derived node ids in startNode/endNode untouched.
# Those arbitrary node ids (e.g. 30, 47) are what convert_channel actually
# uses, so the ordering we imposed on 'name' had no effect.
#
# This script replaces startNode/endNode with fresh topological ids BEFORE
# calling convert_channel.
# =============================================================================

locs <- read_csv("/Users/johnmorgan/Documents/VT Research/HBTopModel/STIC_synthesis/04_Locations/Location_hb_W3.csv") %>% 
  mutate(sensor_name = substr(sensor_name, 4, 5)) %>% 
  filter(sensor_name != "st")

# for project to work, I have to specify projection of vect when converting from df to vect
locs_vect <- vect(locs, 
                   geom=c("longitude", "latitude"), 
                   crs = "+proj=longlat +datum=WGS84")
#reproject coordinates from WGS84 to NAD83 19N, which is the projection of raster
locs_projected <- terra::project(locs_vect, crs1)
plot(locs_projected)

## They've been trying to stop the wave, but the wave don't stop
channel <- vect(w3_streams_vect, crs = crs1) %>% 
  crop(w3_outline) %>% 
    mutate(ID = seq(1, n(), 1))

plot(channel)

ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = channel)+
  geom_spatvector_text(data = channel, aes(label = ID))
channel_trimmed <- channel %>%
  filter(!ID %in% c(1, 4, 9, 5))

ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = channel_trimmed)+
  geom_spatvector_text(data = channel_trimmed, aes(label = ID))

test2 <- terra::aggregate(channel_trimmed)

new_data <- data.frame(ID = 1)
values(test2) <- new_data

ggplot()+
  geom_spatvector(data = test2)+
  geom_spatvector_text(data = test2, aes(label = ID))

test3 <- terra::aggregate(channel_trimmed)

# =============================================================================
# Main function
# =============================================================================

#' Build a topologically correct channel SpatVector for dynatop
#'
#' @param channel_sv       SpatVector of directed channel lines (upstream to downstream).
#' @param split_points_sv  SpatVector of points marking desired HRU boundaries.
#' @param snap_tolerance   Max snap distance in map units. Points beyond this warn.
#' @param node_tolerance   Coordinate precision for merging coincident endpoints.
#' @param default_width    Channel width assigned to all segments.
#' @param default_slope    Slope assigned to all segments.
#'
#' @return List with three elements:
#'   $channel_sv    SpatVector ready for convert_channel. startNode values are
#'                  topological ids (outlet = 1). endNode = downstream id (0 at outlet).
#'   $channel_df    data.frame of the same, for inspection and validation.
#'   $conv_channel  Output of convert_channel — pass directly to dynatop.

build_dynatop_channel <- function(
    channel_sv,
    split_points_sv,
    snap_tolerance = 1,
    node_tolerance = 0.01,
    default_width  = 1,
    default_slope  = 0.001
) {

  # ── 0. CRS alignment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  channel_sf <- st_cast(st_as_sf(channel_sv), "LINESTRING")
  points_sf  <- st_as_sf(split_points_sv)
  if (st_crs(channel_sf) != st_crs(points_sf)) {
    message("CRS mismatch — reprojecting split points.")
    points_sf <- st_transform(points_sf, st_crs(channel_sf))
  }
  message(sprintf("Input: %d line feature(s), %d split point(s).",
                  nrow(channel_sf), nrow(points_sf)))

  # ── 1. Snap split points to nearest position on the channel ──────────────────
  channel_union <- st_union(channel_sf)
  nearest_segs  <- st_nearest_points(points_sf, channel_union)
  snapped_pts   <- st_cast(nearest_segs, "POINT")[seq(2, 2*nrow(points_sf), 2)]

  snap_dists <- as.numeric(st_distance(
    points_sf, st_sf(geometry = snapped_pts), by_element = TRUE
  ))
  far <- which(snap_dists > snap_tolerance)
  if (length(far) > 0)
    warning(sprintf("%d point(s) >%g map units from channel (rows: %s).",
                    length(far), snap_tolerance, paste(far, collapse=",")),
            call.=FALSE)

  # ── 2. Split each line at points that fall on it ──────────────────────────────
  segment_list <- vector("list", nrow(channel_sf))
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(channel_sf))) {
    this_line    <- channel_sf[i, ]
    dist_to_line <- as.numeric(st_distance(st_sf(geometry = snapped_pts), this_line))
    on_this      <- which(dist_to_line <= node_tolerance * 10)

    if (length(on_this) == 0) {
      segment_list[[i]] <- st_sf(geometry = st_geometry(this_line))
    } else {
      pts_u <- st_union(snapped_pts[on_this])
      parts <- tryCatch(
        st_collection_extract(lwgeom::st_split(this_line, pts_u), "LINESTRING"),
        error = function(e) {
          warning(sprintf("Split failed on line %d — kept unsplit.", i), call.=FALSE)
          this_line
        }
      )
      segment_list[[i]] <- st_sf(geometry = st_geometry(parts))
    }
  }

  all_segs <- do.call(rbind, segment_list)
  rownames(all_segs) <- NULL
  n_segs <- nrow(all_segs)
  message(sprintf("After splitting: %d segment(s).", n_segs))

  # ── 3. Build coordinate-based node topology ────────────────────────────────
  # Directed lines: first coordinate = upstream end, last = downstream end.
  precision <- max(0, ceiling(-log10(node_tolerance)) + 1)
  get_endpoints <- function(geom) {
    co <- st_coordinates(geom)
    rbind(round(co[1,        c("X","Y")], precision),
          round(co[nrow(co), c("X","Y")], precision))
  }

  ep_matrix   <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(n_segs), function(i) get_endpoints(all_segs[i,])))
  coord_keys  <- paste(ep_matrix[,"X"], ep_matrix[,"Y"])
  unique_keys <- unique(coord_keys)
  node_ids    <- match(coord_keys, unique_keys)
  start_ids   <- node_ids[seq(1, 2*n_segs, 2)]  # coord-based, arbitrary integers
  end_ids     <- node_ids[seq(2, 2*n_segs, 2)]

  # ── 4. Topological sort — BFS from outlet ────────────────────────────────────
  sn_to_row <- setNames(seq_len(n_segs), as.character(start_ids))
  ds        <- unname(sn_to_row[as.character(end_ids)])  # NA = outlet

  outlets <- which(is.na(ds))
  if (length(outlets) == 0)
    stop(paste0(
      "No outlet found — every segment's end connects to another segment's start.\n",
      "Verify that lines are directed upstream → downstream."
    ))
  if (length(outlets) > 1)
    warning(sprintf(
      "%d outlets detected — network may be disconnected or have unjoined tributaries.",
      length(outlets)
    ), call.=FALSE)

  upstream_of <- vector("list", n_segs)
  for (i in seq_len(n_segs))
    if (!is.na(ds[i])) upstream_of[[ds[i]]] <- c(upstream_of[[ds[i]]], i)

  visited   <- logical(n_segs)
  new_order <- integer(0)
  queue     <- outlets
  while (length(queue) > 0) {
    cur <- queue[1]; queue <- queue[-1]
    if (!visited[cur]) {
      visited[cur] <- TRUE
      new_order    <- c(new_order, cur)
      queue        <- c(queue, upstream_of[[cur]])
    }
  }

  unreachable <- setdiff(seq_len(n_segs), new_order)
  if (length(unreachable) > 0)
    warning(sprintf(
      "%d segment(s) unreachable from the outlet — possible cycle or disconnected sub-network.",
      length(unreachable)
    ), call.=FALSE)

  n_sorted <- length(new_order)

  # ── 5. THE KEY STEP: replace startNode/endNode with topological ids ───────────
  #
  # convert_channel uses startNode as each HRU's id, and endNode as its
  # sf_flow_direction. So these values — not 'name', not row order — must be
  # topologically ordered so that endNode < startNode for every non-outlet.
  #
  # BFS order gives us: new_order[1] = outlet row, new_order[n] = headwaters.
  # Assign topo_id = position in new_order, so outlet gets topo_id = 1.
  # Each segment's downstream row has a LOWER topo_id by construction.

  topo_id   <- seq_len(n_sorted)  # 1 = outlet, n_sorted = farthest headwater

  # Find downstream topo_id for each sorted segment
  sorted_start_orig <- start_ids[new_order]
  sorted_end_orig   <- end_ids[new_order]
  sn_orig_to_sorted_row <- setNames(seq_len(n_sorted), as.character(sorted_start_orig))
  ds_sorted <- unname(sn_orig_to_sorted_row[as.character(sorted_end_orig)])  # NA = outlet

  # New startNode = this segment's topo_id
  # New endNode   = downstream segment's topo_id; 0 for outlet
  new_startNode <- topo_id
  new_endNode   <- ifelse(is.na(ds_sorted), 0L, topo_id[ds_sorted])

  # Guarantee: for all non-outlet, new_endNode < new_startNode
  # Because ds_sorted[i] < i always (BFS visits downstream before upstream)
  violations <- which(new_endNode != 0 & new_endNode >= new_startNode)
  if (length(violations) > 0)
    stop(sprintf(
      "BUG: topological id assignment still has violations at sorted rows: %s.\n",
      paste(violations, collapse=", ")
    ), "Please file a bug report.")

  sorted_lengths <- as.numeric(st_length(all_segs[new_order, ]))

  # ── 6. Assemble output ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  channel_df <- data.frame(
    name      = topo_id,          # label = id for clarity; convert_channel reads this as label only
    startNode = new_startNode,    # THIS is what becomes the HRU id in dynatop
    endNode   = new_endNode,      # THIS is what becomes sf_flow_direction
    length    = sorted_lengths,
    width     = default_width,
    slope     = default_slope,
    area      = sorted_lengths * default_width,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  sorted_geom    <- st_geometry(all_segs[new_order, ])
  channel_sf_out <- st_sf(channel_df, geometry = sorted_geom)
  channel_sv_out <- vect(channel_sf_out)
  crs(channel_sv_out) <- crs(channel_sv)

  # ── 7. Pre-flight validation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
  validate_channel_sv(channel_sv_out, verbose = TRUE)

  # ── 8. Run convert_channel ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  conv_channel <- convert_channel(
    channel_sv_out,
    property_names = c(
      name      = "name",
      length    = "length",
      startNode = "startNode",
      endNode   = "endNode"
    ),
    default_width = default_width,
    default_slope = default_slope
  )

  list(
    channel_sv   = channel_sv_out,
    channel_df   = channel_df,
    conv_channel = conv_channel
  )
}


# =============================================================================
# Validation: checks the rule as convert_channel sees it
#   id = startNode; sf_flow_direction = endNode
#   => endNode must be strictly less than startNode for every non-outlet row
# =============================================================================

#' Validate a channel SpatVector before passing to convert_channel
#'
#' @param sv       SpatVector with startNode, endNode columns.
#' @param verbose  Print results.
#' @return Logical TRUE (pass) / FALSE (fail), invisibly.

validate_channel_sv <- function(sv, verbose = TRUE) {
  df <- as.data.frame(sv)

  outlet_rows <- df$endNode == 0 | !df$endNode %in% df$startNode
  bad         <- which(!outlet_rows & df$endNode >= df$startNode)

  if (verbose) {
    message(sprintf(
      "Segments: %d | Outlet startNode(s): %s | Violations: %d",
      nrow(df),
      paste(df$startNode[outlet_rows], collapse=", "),
      length(bad)
    ))
    if (length(bad) > 0) {
      message("Violations (endNode must be < startNode for non-outlet segments):")
      print(df[bad, c("name","startNode","endNode")], row.names=FALSE)
    } else {
      message("PASS — startNode/endNode ordering is correct for convert_channel and dynatop.")
    }
  }
  invisible(length(bad) == 0)
}


# =============================================================================
# Usage
# =============================================================================
#
result <- build_dynatop_channel(
  channel_sv      = test3,
  split_points_sv = locs_projected,
  snap_tolerance  = 5,
  node_tolerance  = 0.01,
  default_width   = 1,
  default_slope   = 0.001
)

writeVector(
  result$conv_channel,
  file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed", "channel.shp"),
  overwrite = TRUE
)

# # Inspect the final table — startNode IS the HRU id, endNode IS sf_flow_direction
print(result$channel_df)
plot(result$channel_sv)

plot(st_as_sf(result$channel_sv))
#
# # result$conv_channel is already the convert_channel output — use directly:
# ctch_mdl <- dynatop$new(hru)   # where hru includes result$conv_channel
#
# # You can also re-validate at any time:
 validate_channel_sv(result$channel_sv)
 
 ggplot() +
  # Plot the raster base map (using factor for discrete colors)
  geom_spatvector(data = result$channel_sv) +
  geom_spatvector_text(data = result$channel_sv, aes(label = name))
  
  geom_text(data = centroid_coords[4,], aes(x = x, y = y, label = hru_id), 
            color = "black", fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  # Use a nice color palette and remove the bulky legend
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", na.value = "transparent", guide = "none") +
  lims(x = c(281500, 281700),
       y = c(4870400, 4870500))+
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Hubbard Brook HRU & Channel ID Map",
       subtitle = "Hillslopes (1-20) and Channels (21+)",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude")
```

```{r}
sv <- result$channel_sv
# Convert to sf
sv_sf <- st_as_sf(sv)

# Function to merge selected names into single row
#merge_by_names <- function(sv_sf, names_to_merge, new_name) {
names_to_merge <- c(1,2,3,4, 5)
# Keep rows NOT in the merge list
others <- sv_sf[!sv_sf$name %in% names_to_merge, ]
  
  # Combine selected lines
  to_merge <- sv_sf[sv_sf$name %in% names_to_merge, ]
  plot(to_merge)
  merged_geom <- st_union(st_geometry(to_merge))
  plot(merged_geom)
  # New row for merged lines
# figure out rows that will need endNode fixed
new_ends <- sv_sf %>% 
  filter(endNode %in% names_to_merge) %>% 
  filter(!name %in% names_to_merge)


merged_row <- st_sf(
  name = 1,
  startNode = 1,
  endNode = 0,
  length = sum(to_merge$length),
  width = 1,
  slope = 0.001,
  area = sum(to_merge$area),
  geometry = merged_geom
)
  
  # Bind back together and convert
all_rows <- rbind(merged_row, others)
all_rows$endNode[which(all_rows$name %in% new_ends$name)] <- 1

vect_final <- vect(all_rows)
plot(vect_final)

ggplot() +
  # Plot the raster base map (using factor for discrete colors)
  geom_spatvector(data = vect_final) +
  geom_spatvector_text(data = vect_final, aes(label = name))#+
  
  
  # Use a nice color palette and remove the bulky legend
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", na.value = "transparent", guide = "none") +
  lims(x = c(281500, 281700),
       y = c(4870400, 4870500))+
  theme_minimal() 

  
writeVector(
  vect_final,
  file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed", "channel.shp"),
  overwrite = TRUE
)
```


# TIMESERIES
```{r}
start <- "2023-05-21"
end <- "2024-05-31"
```

```{r discharge}


# discharge every 15 mins
flow_15min <- read_csv("https://pasta.lternet.edu/package/data/eml/knb-lter-hbr/1/18/1b101627265d0b5763fa0bba93d93bf7") 

flow_xts <- 
  flow_15min %>%
  filter(DATETIME >= start,
         DATETIME <= end) %>% 
  # Create a new column that groups time into 15-minute buckets
  mutate(time_15 = floor_date(DATETIME, "15 mins")) %>%
  # Group by those buckets
  group_by(time_15) %>%
  # Average the 5-minute instantaneous rates to get the 15-minute rate
  summarize(Discharge_ls = mean(Discharge_ls, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  # convert to m3_s, or should it be m3/day?>
  mutate(Discharge_m3_s = Discharge_ls / 1000) %>% 
  as.xts()

```
Units of precipitation and pet are expected to be m accrued over the previous timestep.
```{r precipitation}
precip <- read_csv("https://pasta.lternet.edu/package/data/eml/knb-lter-hbr/278/7/132ffe0622ff3f30505c774d24a6b65d")
# precip in mm accrued over timestep, needs to be m accrued over time step

precip_xts <- 
  precip %>% 
  filter(DateTime >= start,
         DateTime <= end) %>% 
  select(DateTime, precip) %>% 
  mutate(precip = precip / 1000) %>% # convert to m
  as.xts()
```
```{r pet}
# air temp already 15 min increment
air <- read_csv("https://pasta.lternet.edu/package/data/eml/knb-lter-hbr/322/7/a96172a26f381b9ec7005144d12e0820")

air_formatted <- air %>% 
  filter(DateTime >= start,
         DateTime <= end) %>% 
  select(DateTime, airTemp) 

# convert airTemp to pet with hargreaves

lat <- 45   # latitude in decimal degrees (e.g., Blacksburg VA)

# --- Hargreaves PET -----------------------------------------------------

hargreaves_pet <- function(date, temp, lat){

  J <- yday(date)                 # day of year
  phi <- lat * pi/180             # latitude radians
  
  # extraterrestrial radiation (Ra)
  dr  <- 1 + 0.033*cos(2*pi*J/365)
  delta <- 0.409*sin(2*pi*J/365 - 1.39)
  ws <- acos(-tan(phi)*tan(delta))

  Gsc <- 0.0820   # MJ m-2 min-1
  
  Ra <- (24*60/pi) * Gsc * dr *
        (ws*sin(phi)*sin(delta) +
         cos(phi)*cos(delta)*sin(ws))

  PET <- 0.0023 * Ra *
         (temp + 17.8) *
         sqrt(pmax(temp, 0))

  return(PET)   # mm/day
}

# --- Calculate PET ------------------------------------------------------

air_formatted$PET_mm_day <- hargreaves_pet(
  air_formatted$DateTime,
  air_formatted$airTemp,
  lat
)


pet_xts <- air_formatted %>% 
  select(DateTime, PET_mm_day, airTemp) %>% 
  mutate(PET_m_day = PET_mm_day / (1000 * 96)) %>% # convert to m, and 15 min
  rename(pet = PET_m_day) %>% 
  select(DateTime, pet, airTemp) %>% 
  as.xts()

 
```
```{r combine}

obs <- merge(flow_xts[,2], # flow does not go beyond 2024-05-31 23:45:00
             precip_xts,
             pet_xts,
             all=FALSE) ## merge by time stamp
#obs <- obs[ , colSums(is.na(obs)) == 0]               # Remove columns with at least one NA

# Save it
saveRDS(obs, file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed", "obs.rds"))

obs <- readRDS(file.path(".", "hb_data", "processed", "obs.rds"))
names(obs) <- c("Discharge_m3_s", "precip","pet","temp_series" )

#saveRDS(obs, file.path(".", "processed", "obs.rds"))
```

# CREATE DYNATOP OBJECT
```{r}
ctch <- dynatopGIS$new(file.path(".", "dynaGISgoodChannel"))
# add catchment, DEM, channel
ctch$add_catchment( "./hb_data/processed/w3.tif")
ctch$add_dem( "./hb_data/processed/dem.tif")
ctch$add_channel( "./hb_data/processed/channel.shp")

ctch$get_layer()

# plot just to check it out!
ctch$plot_layer("dem", add_channel=TRUE)
```

```{r fill-sinks}
ctch$sink_fill()

terra::plot( ctch$get_layer('filled_dem') - ctch$get_layer('dem'),
             main="Changes to height")
```

```{r compute-stuff}
# bands
ctch$compute_band()
ctch$plot_layer("band")
# twi
ctch$compute_properties()
ctch$plot_layer('atb')
# flow lengths
ctch$compute_flow_lengths(flow_routing="shortest")
ctch$plot_layer("shortest_flow_length")
```

```{r classifying and cutting}
## simple equally spaced cuts
ctch$classify("atb_20","atb",20)
ctch$plot_layer("atb_20")

## use the quantile function of the raster package to compute breaks
## note we need to include the min and max
brk <- global(ctch$get_layer("atb"), fun=quantile,probs = seq(0, 1, length = 21),na.rm=T)
ctch$classify("atb_20_equal","atb",brk)
ctch$plot_layer("atb_20_equal")

ctch$combine_classes("atb_20_subcatch",c("atb_20","catchment"))
ctch$plot_layer("atb_20_subcatch")

ctch$combine_classes("atb_20_subcatch_band",c("atb_20_subcatch","band"))
ctch$plot_layer("atb_20_subcatch_band")
```

```{r create the model}
ctch$create_model(file.path("./dynaGISgoodChannel","atb_20"), # name of new model
                  "atb_20", # classification to base model on
                  sf_opt="cnst",
                  sz_opt="exp",
                  )

## load the model
mdl <- readRDS( file.path(".","dynaGISgoodChannel","atb_20.rds")) ## read the model in
#hru <- mdl$hru
names(mdl)
```

# RUN MODEL
```{r add snow parameters to model object}
# 2. Patch the HRUs with snow parameters, temp linkages, and expanded states
for (i in seq_along(mdl$hru)) {
  
  # A. Add the snow parameters
  mdl$hru[[i]]$snow <- list(
    type = "hbv",
    parameters = c(T_T = 0.0, C_f = 2.5, C_wh = 0.1)
  )
  
  # B. Add the temp linkage 
  # Note: The original precip linkage uses 'name' and 'fraction'
  # We will map it to a dummy "temp_series"
  mdl$hru[[i]]$temp <- list(
    name = "temp_series",
    fraction = 1.0
  )
  
  # C. Expand the states to include s_snow and s_liquid
  mdl$hru[[i]]$states <- c(
    mdl$hru[[i]]$states, 
    s_snow = 0, 
    s_liquid = 0
  )
}

# fpar <- function(h){ ## h is the hru
#     ## test if a channel
#     if( !is.na(h$class$startNode) ){
#         ## change type
#         h$sf$type <- "cnst"
#         h$sf$parameters["csf"] <- 1
#     }
#     h
# }
# mdl$hru <- lapply(mdl$hru, fpar)

for(ii in seq_along(mdl$hru)){
  is_channel <- !is.na(mdl$hru[[ii]]$class$endNode)
  if(is_channel && mdl$hru[[ii]]$sf$type == "kin"){
    mdl$hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["t_raf"] <- 0
    mdl$hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["s_raf"] <- 0
  }
}

```

```{r plot HRUS}

hru_map <- rast(mdl$map) 

# Rename the raster layer so we can call it easily in ggplot
names(hru_map) <- "hru_id"

# 3. Convert the raster to polygons to calculate the center of each HRU
hru_poly <- as.polygons(hru_map)

# Calculate the centroids (center points) of each polygon for the text labels
hru_centroids <- centroids(hru_poly)

# Extract coordinates into a standard dataframe for ggplot
centroid_coords <- crds(hru_centroids, df = TRUE)
centroid_coords$hru_id <- hru_centroids$hru_id
              # 4. Plot using tidyterra and ggplot2
ggplot() +
  # Plot the raster base map (using factor for discrete colors)
  geom_spatraster(data = as.factor(hru_map)) +
  # Overlay the polygon boundaries
  #geom_spatvector(data = hru_poly, fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.2) +
  # Add the ID labels at the center of each HRU
  geom_text(data = centroid_coords[4,], aes(x = x, y = y, label = hru_id), 
            color = "black", fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  # Use a nice color palette and remove the bulky legend
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", na.value = "transparent", guide = "none") +
  lims(x = c(281500, 281700),
       y = c(4870400, 4870500))+
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Hubbard Brook HRU & Channel ID Map",
       subtitle = "Hillslopes (1-20) and Channels (21+)",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude")

```

```{r test run model}
# Load previously created data
output_defn <- data.frame(name = c("q_sf", "v_sf_rz"),
                      id = 0,
                      flux = c("s_sf", "v_sf_rz"),
                      scale = c(1, -1/900))
#mdl <- readRDS(file.path(".","dynaGIS","atb_20.rds"))
#obs <- readRDS(file.path(".","processed","obs.rds"))
# initialize dynatop object

## determining the initial recharge rate ad setting it in the model
# I think the same number is the watershed area...
#r0 <- as.numeric(obs[1, "Streamflow"])#/ 86400
#r0 <- as.numeric(obs$Streamflow[1])
 r0 <- as.numeric(obs$Discharge_m3_s[1])#/ sum( sapply( hru, function(x){x$properties["area"]} ) )
hru <- lapply(hru,function(h){h$initialisation["r_uz_sz_0"] <- r0;h})

## the map input is only needed for visualisation
ctch_mdl <- dynatop$new(mdl$hru, map = mdl$map
  )$add_data( obs
  )$initialise(
  )$sim(mdl$output_flux
  )


# Gauge Flows
sim_gauge <- ctch_mdl$get_output() ## extract the flow as an xts object
head(sim_gauge)
plot(sim_gauge[,1],main="Simulated and observed flows",
     legend.loc='topright')
plot(merge(sim_gauge, obs$Discharge_m3_s),main="Simulated and observed flows",
     legend.loc='topright')


new_mdl <- ctch_mdl$get_model() ## get the model structure from the dynatop object
new_mdl[[1]]

```

# PARAMETERIZE
```{r working param, but we can do better}
# =============================================================================
# dynatop Multi-Objective Calibration
# Objectives: (1) Discharge fit via KGE
#             (2) Flow presence/absence at channel observation points via CSI
# =============================================================================

# =============================================================================
# 1. LOAD YOUR MODEL AND DATA
# =============================================================================
# fix these later
# mdl <- readRDS(file.path(".", "dynaGIS", "atb_20.rds"))
# obs <- readRDS(file.path(".", "processed", "obs.rds"))

# obs must have columns: precip (m/step), pet (m/step), temp (°C), 
#                        and your observed discharge column
# Units should already be fixed (precip/pet in m, not mm)

# Observed discharge time series (m³/s) — used for KGE

cal_start <- as.POSIXct("2023-05-15", tz = "UTC")
cal_end   <- as.POSIXct("2024-05-31", tz = "UTC")
obs_cal   <- obs[index(obs) >= cal_start & index(obs) <= cal_end, ]
obs_q <- obs$Discharge_m3_s
obs_q <- obs[index(obs) >= cal_start & index(obs) <= cal_end, ]$Discharge_m3_s
#actual <- obs$Discharge_m3_s

# =============================================================================
# 2. FLOW PRESENCE/ABSENCE OBSERVATIONS
# =============================================================================
# Format: data frame with columns:
#   hru_id   — integer, which HRU to extract q_sf from (0-indexed, matching model)
#   datetime — POSIXct
#   observed — integer, 1 = flowing, 0 = dry
#
# If your observations are date ranges rather than individual timesteps, 
# use the helper below to expand them first.

# ── Option A: already have a long data frame ─────────────────────────────────
# pa_obs <- read.csv("presence_absence.csv")
# pa_obs$datetime <- as.POSIXct(pa_obs$datetime, tz = "UTC")
all_24 <- read_csv("/Users/johnmorgan/Documents/VT Research/HBTopModel/STIC_Synthesis/all_24.csv")


stic_locs <- all_24 %>% 
  filter(wshed == "W3") %>% 
  select(number, lat, long) %>% 
  unique() %>% 
  vect(geom = c("long", "lat"), crs = "EPSG:4326") %>% 
  project(.,"EPSG:26919")




dist_table <- tibble(distance(result$conv_channel, stic_locs))

for(i in 1:length(stic_locs$number)){
  stic <- stic_locs[i,]
  dist_table <- (distance(stic_locs[i,], result$conv_channel))
  index <- unname(which(dist_table == min(dist_table), arr.ind = TRUE)[,2])
  
  output <- tibble(ID = stic$number,
                   channel_ID = result$conv_channel[index,]$name,
                   
                   dist = min(dist_table))
  if(i == 1) all_out <- output
  if(i > 1) all_out <- rbind(all_out, output)
}

chosen <- all_out %>% 
  filter(dist <= 30)
pa_obs <- 
  all_24 %>% 
  filter(wshed == "W3", number %in% chosen$ID) %>% 
  select(datetime, number, wetdry) %>% 
  rename(ID = number) %>% 
  left_join(chosen, multiple = "first") %>% 
  mutate(observed = as.integer(case_when(wetdry == "wet" ~ 1,
                            wetdry == "dry" ~ 0)),
         hru_id = as.integer(channel_ID),
         datetime = as.POSIXct(datetime)) %>% 
  select(hru_id, datetime, observed) %>% 
  filter(datetime > start,
         datetime < end)

# ── Option B: expand date ranges to 15-min timesteps ─────────────────────────
# Adapt this table to your actual observations:
# pa_ranges <- data.frame(
#   hru_id   = c(3L,  3L,  7L,  7L),   # HRU ids matching model (check with outlet loop)
#   location = c("site_A", "site_A", "site_B", "site_B"),
#   start    = as.POSIXct(c("2017-05-21", "2017-06-15",
#                            "2017-06-01", "2017-07-01"), tz = "UTC"),
#   end      = as.POSIXct(c("2017-06-14", "2017-07-21",
#                            "2017-06-30", "2017-07-21"), tz = "UTC"),
#   observed = c(1L, 0L, 1L, 0L)  # 1=flowing, 0=dry during that range
# )

# expand_pa_ranges <- function(ranges, obs_index){
#   # obs_index: the index() of your obs xts object
#   do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(ranges)), function(i){
#     r   <- ranges[i, ]
#     idx <- obs_index[obs_index >= r$start & obs_index <= r$end]
#     if(length(idx) == 0) return(NULL)
#     data.frame(hru_id   = r$hru_id,
#                location = r$location,
#                datetime = idx,
#                observed = r$observed,
#                stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
#   }))
# }
# 
# pa_obs <- expand_pa_ranges(pa_ranges, index(obs))

# Threshold: simulated q_sf (m³/s) above which channel is "flowing"
# Tune this to the detection limit of your field observations
FLOW_THRESHOLD <- 1e-06  # m³/s — adjust to your watershed scale

# =============================================================================
# 3. PARAMETER SPACE DEFINITION
# =============================================================================
# Each row: parameter name, lower bound, upper bound
# Adjust ranges to your watershed — these are reasonable starting points
# for a mineral soil catchment

param_bounds <- data.frame(
  name  = c("t_0",      # saturated zone transmissivity at saturation (m²/s)
             "m",        # exponential decay constant (m⁻¹)
             "t_d",      # unsaturated zone time delay (s)
             "s_rzmax",  # root zone max storage (m)
             "c_sf",     # hillslope surface velocity (m/s) — if cnst sf type
             "v_ch",     # channel surface velocity (m/s)
             "T_T",     # snow/rain temperature threshold (°C)
             "C_f",  # degree-day melt factor (m/°C/day)
             "C_wh"),  # snowfall correction factor (-)
  lower = c(0.001,  0.005,  0.1,   0.05,  0.1,  0.3, 0.0,  0.001,  0.5),
  upper = c(5.0,    0.08,   8000,  0.5,   1.0,  2.0,  2.0,  0.008,  1.5),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
n_params <- nrow(param_bounds)

# =============================================================================
# 4. MODEL RUNNER
# =============================================================================
# Takes a named parameter vector, sets it in the model, runs one simulation,
# returns a list with simulated discharge xts and the full sim output

#output_defn
#ctch_mdl <- dynatop$new(Swindale$model$hru)$add_data(obs[,-1])$initialise()$sim(output_defn)

test_params <- setNames(
  (param_bounds$lower + param_bounds$upper) / 2,
  param_bounds$name
)
#Swindale$model$map <- "./hb_data/dynaGIS/atb_20.tif"


run_model <- function(params, mdl_template, obs,
                      outlet_id   = 0L,
                      pa_hru_ids  = unique(pa_obs$hru_id),
                      sub_step    = 900){
  
  hru <- mdl_template$hru
  
  for(ii in seq_along(hru)){
    is_channel <- !is.na(hru[[ii]]$class$endNode)
    
    if(!is_channel){
      # ── Hillslope ──
      hru[[ii]]$sz$parameters["t_0"]     <- params["t_0"]
      hru[[ii]]$sz$parameters["m"]       <- params["m"]
      hru[[ii]]$uz$parameters["t_d"]     <- params["t_d"]
      hru[[ii]]$rz$parameters["s_rzmax"] <- params["s_rzmax"]
      
      # Surface type determines which parameter to set:
      if(hru[[ii]]$sf$type == "cnst"){
        hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["c_sf"] <- params["c_sf"]
      } else if(hru[[ii]]$sf$type == "kin"){
        # kin type uses n (Manning) — optionally add "n" to param_bounds
        # hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["n"] <- params["n"]
      }
      
    } else {
      # ── Channel ──
      hru[[ii]]$sz$parameters["t_0"]     <- 0.001          # keep low
      hru[[ii]]$rz$parameters["s_rzmax"] <- 0.001
      if(hru[[ii]]$sf$type == "cnst"){
        hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["c_sf"]  <- params["v_ch"]
      }
    }
    
    # ── Snow parameters (all HRUs) ──
    hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters["T_T"]    <- params["T_T"]
    hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters["C_f"] <- params["C_f"]
    hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters["C_wh"]  <- params["C_wh"]
    
    # ── Initialisation ──
    total_area <- sum(sapply(hru, function(h) h$properties["area"]))
    baseflow   <- as.numeric(obs$Discharge_m3_s[1])
    hru[[ii]]$initialisation["s_rz_0"]    <- 0.5
    hru[[ii]]$initialisation["r_uz_sz_0"] <- baseflow / total_area
  }
  
  # Build output definition: outlet discharge + presence/absence HRUs
  pa_ids   <- unique(pa_hru_ids)
  out_rows <- rbind(
  data.frame(name  = "q_out",
             id    = 0L,
             flux  = "q_sf",
             scale = 1,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  data.frame(name  = paste0("q_sf_hru", pa_ids),
             id    = pa_ids,
             flux  = "q_sf",
             scale = 1,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
)
  
  # Run
  result <- tryCatch({
    ctch_mdl <- dynatop$new(hru, map = mdl_template$map)
    ctch_mdl$add_data(obs)
    ctch_mdl$initialise()
    ctch_mdl$sim(out_rows, sub_step = sub_step)$get_output()
  # }, error = function(e){
  #   NULL  # return NULL on failure — objective will return penalty
  })
  
  return(result)
}
test_parms <- data.frame(
  name = param_bounds$name,
  value = c(exp(7.46), # t_0
            0.00698, # m
            8*60*60, # t_d
            0.1, # s_rzmax
            0.5425, #c_sf
            1, # v_ch
            0, # T_T
            2.5, # C_f
            0.1 # C_wh
            )
)
test_params <- setNames(
  test_parms$value,
  test_parms$name
)
test_run <- run_model(test_params,
          mdl,
          obs,
          outlet_id = 0L
          )
plot(test_run$q_out)
# =============================================================================
# 5. OBJECTIVE FUNCTION COMPONENTS
# =============================================================================

# ── KGE (Kling-Gupta Efficiency) for discharge ───────────────────────────────
# KGE = 1 - sqrt((r-1)² + (α-1)² + (β-1)²)
# r = correlation, α = std ratio, β = mean ratio
# Returns value in (-Inf, 1]; 1 is perfect; > 0.5 is generally "good"
kge <- function(sim, obs){
  # align by time index
  both  <- merge(sim, obs, join = "inner")
  s     <- as.numeric(both[, 1])
  o     <- as.numeric(both[, 2])
  valid <- is.finite(s) & is.finite(o) & abs(s) < 1e6  # ADD abs check here
  if(sum(valid) < 10) return(-Inf)
  s <- s[valid]; o <- o[valid]
  r   <- cor(s, o)
  a   <- sd(s) / sd(o)
  b   <- mean(s) / mean(o)
  1 - sqrt((r - 1)^2 + (a - 1)^2 + (b - 1)^2)
}

# ── NSE alternative (uncomment to swap in) ───────────────────────────────────
nse <- function(sim, obs){
  both  <- merge(sim, obs, join = "inner")
  s     <- as.numeric(both[, 1])
  o     <- as.numeric(both[, 2])
  valid <- is.finite(s) & is.finite(o)
  if(sum(valid) < 10) return(-Inf)
  s <- s[valid]; o <- o[valid]
  1 - sum((s - o)^2) / sum((o - mean(o))^2)
}

# ── Critical Success Index (CSI) for presence/absence ────────────────────────
# CSI = TP / (TP + FP + FN)  — ranges 0 to 1; 1 is perfect
# Also called Threat Score. Penalises both false positives and false negatives.
# Alternatives: F1 = 2TP/(2TP+FP+FN), MCC (Matthews Correlation Coefficient)
csi_presence_absence <- function(sim_output, pa_obs, threshold = FLOW_THRESHOLD){
  
  TP <- 0L; FP <- 0L; FN <- 0L; TN <- 0L
  
  for(hid in unique(pa_obs$hru_id)){
    
    col_name <- paste0("q_sf_hru", hid)
    if(!col_name %in% names(sim_output)) next
    
    sim_q   <- sim_output[, col_name]
    sub_obs <- pa_obs[pa_obs$hru_id == hid, ]
    sub_obs_xts <- xts(sub_obs$observed,
                       order.by = as.POSIXct(sub_obs$datetime, tz = "UTC"))
    
    both    <- merge(sim_q, sub_obs_xts, join = "inner")
    if(nrow(both) == 0) next
    
    sim_bin <- as.integer(as.numeric(both[, 1]) >= threshold)
    obs_bin <- as.integer(as.numeric(both[, 2]))
    valid   <- !is.na(sim_bin) & !is.na(obs_bin)
    sim_bin <- sim_bin[valid]; obs_bin <- obs_bin[valid]
    
    TP <- TP + sum(sim_bin == 1L & obs_bin == 1L)
    FP <- FP + sum(sim_bin == 1L & obs_bin == 0L)
    FN <- FN + sum(sim_bin == 0L & obs_bin == 1L)
    TN <- TN + sum(sim_bin == 0L & obs_bin == 0L)
  }
  
  denom <- TP + FP + FN
  if(denom == 0) return(NA_real_)
  
  list(
    CSI      = TP / denom,
    F1       = if((2*TP + FP + FN) > 0) 2*TP / (2*TP + FP + FN) else 0,
    accuracy = (TP + TN) / (TP + FP + FN + TN),
    TP = TP, FP = FP, FN = FN, TN = TN
  )
}

# =============================================================================
# 6. COMBINED OBJECTIVE FUNCTION
# =============================================================================
# Returns a single value to MINIMISE (so we negate KGE and CSI).
# w_kge + w_csi should sum to 1. Adjust weights to reflect your priorities.

W_KGE <- 1   # weight on discharge KGE
W_CSI <- 0   # weight on presence/absence CSI

PENALTY <- 100  # returned on model crash or non-finite result

objective <- function(param_vec, mdl_template, obs, pa_obs,
                      outlet_id = 1L, verbose = FALSE){
  
  params <- setNames(param_vec, param_bounds$name)
  
  sim <- run_model(params, mdl_template, obs, outlet_id,
                   pa_hru_ids = unique(pa_obs$hru_id))
  
  # Guard: reject obviously unstable parameter combinations before running
  if(params["s_rzmax"] < params["t_d"] / 1e8){  # t_d too large relative to rzmax
    return(PENALTY)
  }
  
  if(is.null(sim)) return(PENALTY)
  
  # Check for numerical blow-up in output
  sim_vals <- as.numeric(sim)
  if(any(!is.finite(sim_vals))) return(PENALTY)
  if(any(abs(sim_vals) > 1e6))  return(PENALTY)  # catches blow-up that is still finite
  
  if(is.null(sim)){
    if(verbose) cat("Model run failed — returning penalty\n")
    return(PENALTY)
  }
  
  # ── Discharge objective ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  sim_q   <- sim[, "q_out"]
  kge_val <- kge(sim_q, obs_q)
  if(!is.finite(kge_val)) return(PENALTY)
  
  # ── Presence/absence objective ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  pa_scores <- csi_presence_absence(sim, pa_obs)
  csi_val   <- if(!is.null(pa_scores) && is.finite(pa_scores$CSI)) pa_scores$CSI else 0
  
  # ── Combined (minimise negative weighted sum) ──────────────────────────────
  combined <- -(W_KGE * kge_val + W_CSI * csi_val)
  
  if(verbose){
    cat(sprintf("KGE=%.3f  CSI=%.3f  TP=%d FP=%d FN=%d  Combined=%.3f\n",
                kge_val, csi_val,
                pa_scores$TP, pa_scores$FP, pa_scores$FN,
                -combined))
  }
  
  return(combined)
}

# =============================================================================
# GLUE — Generalized Likelihood Uncertainty Estimation
# Replaces the run_lhs_calibration and DEoptim sections
# =============================================================================

# ── Likelihood functions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# GLUE typically uses informal likelihoods. We use KGE and CSI directly,
# combined as a weighted product. Both range 0-1 so the product is also 0-1.
# Runs below the behavioural threshold are rejected (likelihood = 0).

KGE_THRESHOLD <- 0.3   # minimum acceptable KGE — adjust based on your results
CSI_THRESHOLD <- 0.0   # minimum acceptable CSI — raise once model is working

glue_likelihood <- function(kge_val, csi_val){
  # Reject non-behavioural runs
  if(!is.finite(kge_val) || kge_val < KGE_THRESHOLD) return(0)
  if(!is.finite(csi_val) || csi_val < CSI_THRESHOLD) return(0)
  
  # Informal likelihood: weighted product
  # Rescale KGE from [threshold, 1] to [0, 1] before combining
  kge_scaled <- (kge_val - KGE_THRESHOLD) / (1 - KGE_THRESHOLD)
  
  L <- (kge_scaled ^ W_KGE) * (csi_val ^ W_CSI)
  return(L)
}

# ── GLUE sampler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
run_glue <- function(n_samples,
                     mdl_template,
                     obs,
                     pa_obs,
                     outlet_id   = 1L,
                     n_cores     = 1L,
                     seed        = 42L){
  set.seed(seed)
  
  # LHS design scaled to parameter bounds (same as before)
  lhs_design   <- randomLHS(n_samples, n_params)
  param_matrix <- sweep(lhs_design, 2, param_bounds$upper - param_bounds$lower, "*")
  param_matrix <- sweep(param_matrix, 2, param_bounds$lower, "+")
  colnames(param_matrix) <- param_bounds$name
  
  cat(sprintf("Running %d GLUE samples...\n", n_samples))
  
  # ── Worker function — returns full scores not just combined ────────────────
  glue_eval <- function(param_vec){
    params <- setNames(param_vec, param_bounds$name)
    
    # Parameter guards, remove t_d one
    
    sim <- run_model(params, mdl_template, obs, outlet_id,
                     pa_hru_ids = unique(pa_obs$hru_id))
    
    if(is.null(sim)) return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))
    
    sim_vals <- as.numeric(sim)
    if(any(!is.finite(sim_vals)) || any(abs(sim_vals) > 1e6)){
      return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))
    }
    
    kge_val <- kge(sim[, "q_out"], obs_q)
    pa_scores <- csi_presence_absence(sim, pa_obs)
    csi_val <- if(!is.null(pa_scores)) pa_scores$CSI else 0
    
    L <- glue_likelihood(kge_val, csi_val)
    
    list(L = L, kge = kge_val, csi = csi_val)
  }
  
  # ── Run in parallel or serial ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  if(n_cores > 1L){
    library(parallel)
    cl <- makeCluster(n_cores)
    clusterExport(cl,
      c("mdl_template", "obs", "obs_q", "pa_obs", "param_bounds",
        "FLOW_THRESHOLD", "W_KGE", "W_CSI", "KGE_THRESHOLD", "CSI_THRESHOLD",
        "outlet_id", "run_model", "kge", "csi_presence_absence",
        "glue_likelihood", "glue_eval"),
      envir = environment())
    clusterEvalQ(cl, { library(dynatop); library(xts) })
    
    score_list <- parApply(cl, param_matrix, 1, glue_eval)
    stopCluster(cl)
  } else {
    score_list <- apply(param_matrix, 1, glue_eval)
  }
  
  # ── Assemble results ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  results <- data.frame(
    param_matrix,
    L   = sapply(score_list, `[[`, "L"),
    kge = sapply(score_list, `[[`, "kge"),
    csi = sapply(score_list, `[[`, "csi")
  )
  
  n_behavioural <- sum(results$L > 0)
  cat(sprintf("Behavioural runs: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
              n_behavioural, n_samples, 100 * n_behavioural / n_samples))
  
  if(n_behavioural == 0){
    cat("No behavioural runs — lower KGE_THRESHOLD or check model setup.\n")
  }
  
  # Normalise likelihoods to sum to 1 (for uncertainty bounds)
  results$L_norm <- results$L / sum(results$L)
  
  return(results[order(-results$L), ])  # best likelihood first
}

# ── GLUE uncertainty bounds on discharge ──────────────────────────────────────
glue_uncertainty_bounds <- function(glue_results, mdl_template, obs,
                                    outlet_id = 0L,
                                    quantiles = c(0.05, 0.95),
                                    n_cores   = 1L){
  
  behav <- glue_results[glue_results$L > 0, ]
  if(nrow(behav) == 0) stop("No behavioural runs to compute bounds from.")
  cat(sprintf("Computing uncertainty bounds from %d behavioural runs...\n",
              nrow(behav)))
  
  # Re-run each behavioural set and collect discharge series
  get_sim_q <- function(i){
    params <- as.numeric(behav[i, param_bounds$name])
    names(params) <- param_bounds$name
    sim <- run_model(params, mdl_template, obs, outlet_id)
    if(is.null(sim)) return(NULL)
    as.numeric(sim[, "q_out"])
  }
  
  sim_matrix <- do.call(cbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(behav)), get_sim_q))
  
  # Weighted quantiles using normalised likelihoods
  L_w <- behav$L_norm / sum(behav$L_norm)  # re-normalise over behavioural only
  
  n_t <- nrow(sim_matrix)
  bounds <- data.frame(
    datetime = index(obs),
    q_obs    = as.numeric(obs_q),
    q_best   = sim_matrix[, 1],  # highest likelihood run
    q_mean   = apply(sim_matrix, 1, weighted.mean, w = L_w)
  )
  for(q in quantiles){
    col_name <- paste0("q_", gsub("\\.", "", sprintf("%.0f", q * 100)))
    bounds[[col_name]] <- apply(sim_matrix, 1, function(row){
      # Weighted quantile
      ord <- order(row)
      cumw <- cumsum(L_w[ord])
      row[ord][which(cumw >= q)[1]]
    })
  }
  return(bounds)
}

# ── Plot GLUE uncertainty bands ───────────────────────────────────────────────
plot_glue_bounds <- function(bounds){
  bounds_xts <- xts(bounds[, -1], order.by = bounds$datetime)
  
  plot(bounds_xts[, "q_obs"], col = "black", lwd = 1.5,
       main = "GLUE uncertainty bounds vs observed discharge [m³/s]",
       ylab = "m³/s")
  
  # Shade uncertainty band
  t_idx <- bounds$datetime
  polygon(c(t_idx, rev(t_idx)),
          c(bounds$q_05, rev(bounds$q_95)),
          col = adjustcolor("steelblue", alpha.f = 0.3), border = NA)
  
  lines(t_idx, bounds$q_best, col = "firebrick", lwd = 1.5)
  lines(t_idx, bounds$q_mean, col = "steelblue", lwd = 1, lty = 2)
  
  legend("topright",
         legend = c("Observed", "Best likelihood", "Weighted mean", "5–95% band"),
         col    = c("black", "firebrick", "steelblue", 
                    adjustcolor("steelblue", 0.3)),
         lwd    = c(1.5, 1.5, 1, 8),
         lty    = c(1, 1, 2, 1))
}

# ── Dotty plots: likelihood vs each parameter ─────────────────────────────────
plot_glue_dotty <- function(glue_results){
  behav    <- glue_results[glue_results$L > 0, ]
  nonbehav <- glue_results[glue_results$L == 0, ]
  
  par(mfrow = c(3, 3))
  for(pn in param_bounds$name){
    plot(nonbehav[[pn]], nonbehav$L,
         xlab = pn, ylab = "Likelihood",
         pch = 16, cex = 0.4, col = "grey70",
         ylim = c(0, max(glue_results$L, na.rm = TRUE)),
         xlim = range(param_bounds[param_bounds$name == pn, c("lower","upper")]))
    points(behav[[pn]], behav$L,
           pch = 16, cex = 0.6, col = "firebrick")
    abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "grey50")
  }
  par(mfrow = c(1,1))
}

# =============================================================================
# RUN GLUE
# =============================================================================
Sys.time()
glue_results <- run_glue(
  n_samples    = 1,        # GLUE needs more samples than optimisation
  mdl_template = mdl,
  obs          = obs_cal,
  pa_obs       = pa_obs,
  outlet_id    = 0L,
  n_cores      = 7L,
  seed         = 42L
)
Sys.time()
saveRDS(glue_results, "glue_results_0l.rds")

# Inspect results
cat("\nTop 10 behavioural runs:\n")
print(head(glue_results[glue_results$L > 0, 
           c(param_bounds$name, "L", "kge", "csi")], 10))

# Dotty plots
plot_glue_dotty(glue_results)

# Uncertainty bounds (re-runs behavioural sets — can be slow)
bounds <- glue_uncertainty_bounds(glue_results, mdl, obs_cal[,-1],
                                  outlet_id = OUTLET_ID)
plot_glue_bounds(bounds)

# =============================================================================
# 8. RUN CALIBRATION (choose one or both)
# =============================================================================

# ── Confirm outlet HRU id ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for(ii in seq_along(mdl$hru)){
  h <- mdl$hru[[ii]]
  if(length(h$sf_flow_direction$id) == 0 & length(h$sz_flow_direction$id) == 0){
    cat("Outlet HRU id:", h$id, "| is channel:", !is.na(h$class$endNode), "\n")
  }
}
OUTLET_ID <- 0L   # ← set from the output above

# ── Quick single-run test before launching calibration ───────────────────────
test_params <- setNames(
  (param_bounds$lower + param_bounds$upper) / 2,   # midpoint of each range
  param_bounds$name
)
cat("Testing single run with midpoint parameters...\n")
test_score <- objective(test_params, mdl, obs[,-1], pa_obs,
                        outlet_id = OUTLET_ID, verbose = TRUE)
cat("Test score:", test_score, "\n")
# If this returns PENALTY (100), check run_model() output manually

cal_start <- as.POSIXct("2023-05-15", tz = "UTC")
cal_start <- min(index(obs_cal))
cal_end <- max(index(obs_cal))


cal_end   <- as.POSIXct("2024-08-31", tz = "UTC")
obs_cal   <- obs[index(obs) >= cal_start & index(obs) <= cal_end, ]

# Also subset pa_obs to the same period
pa_obs_cal <- pa_obs[pa_obs$datetime >= cal_start & 
                     pa_obs$datetime <= cal_end, ]


# =============================================================================
# 9. EVALUATE AND PLOT BEST PARAMETER SET
# =============================================================================

best_params <- as.numeric(glue_results[1, param_bounds$name])
names(best_params) <- param_bounds$name

# Re-run with verbose output to see score breakdown
cat("\nFinal evaluation of best parameters:\n")
objective(best_params, mdl, obs_cal, pa_obs,
          outlet_id = OUTLET_ID, verbose = TRUE)

# Get full simulation output for plotting
best_sim <- run_model(best_params, mdl, obs_cal,
                      outlet_id  = OUTLET_ID,
                      pa_hru_ids = unique(pa_obs$hru_id))

# ── Plot discharge ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot(merge(best_sim[, "q_out"], obs_cal[,2]),
     main = "Observed vs Simulated discharge [m³/s]",
     legend.loc = "topright",
     col = c("firebrick", "black"))


# ── Plot presence/absence performance at each site ────────────────────────────
#for(hid in unique(pa_obs$hru_id)){
  hid <- 37
  col_name <- paste0("q_sf_hru", hid)
  if(!col_name %in% names(best_sim)) next
  
  sim_q <- best_sim[, col_name]
  sub   <- pa_obs[pa_obs$hru_id == hid, ]
  
  # Convert to binary
  sim_bin <- as.integer(as.numeric(sim_q) >= FLOW_THRESHOLD)
  
  plot(sim_q, main = paste("HRU", hid, "— simulated q_sf"),
       ylab = "m³/s", col = "steelblue")
  abline(h = FLOW_THRESHOLD, lty = 2, col = "grey50")
  
  # Shade observed flowing periods
  flowing_periods <- sub[sub$observed == 1, ]
  #if(nrow(flowing_periods) > 0){
    # Group into contiguous blocks
    times <- sort(unique(as.POSIXct(flowing_periods$datetime)))
    rect(xleft  = head(times, -1),
         xright = tail(times, -1),
         ybottom = 0, ytop = par("usr")[4],
         col = adjustcolor("green", alpha.f = 0.15), border = NA)
  }
  legend("topright", legend = c("Simulated", "Flow threshold", "Observed flowing"),
         col = c("steelblue", "grey50", adjustcolor("green", 0.4)),
         lty = c(1, 2, NA), pch = c(NA, NA, 15))
}

# ── Dotty plots: parameter sensitivity from LHS ───────────────────────────────
# Shows which parameters are most constrained by the data
par(mfrow = c(3, 3))
for(pn in param_bounds$name){
  plot(lhs_results[[pn]], -lhs_results$score,
       xlab = pn, ylab = "KGE+CSI combined",
       ylim= c(-1,1),
       pch = 16, cex = 0.5,
       col = ifelse(-lhs_results$score > quantile(-lhs_results$score, 0.9),
                    "firebrick", "grey60"))
  abline(v = best_params[pn], col = "black", lty = 2)
}
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# =============================================================================
# 10. SNOW PARAMETER NOTES
# =============================================================================
# The three snow parameters interact:
#   t_tt   — separates rain from snow events; affects WHICH timesteps accumulate
#   t_cfmax — controls melt RATE; very sensitive in spring
#   t_sfcf  — corrects for wind undercatch of snowfall; effectively a precip
#              multiplier for cold periods; try 0.6-1.2 for most gauges
#
# If your model has SWE observations, you can add a third objective:
#   swe_rmse <- function(sim_swe, obs_swe){ sqrt(mean((sim_swe - obs_swe)^2)) }
# and add W_SWE * (1 - swe_rmse/max_swe) to the combined objective.
#
# Snow parameters are most identifiable in years with clear accumulation +
# melt seasons — consider calibrating only on those years first, then
# fixing snow params and calibrating hydrology on the full record.
```


```{r diagnosing param}
for(hid in unique(pa_obs$hru_id)){
  idx <- which(sapply(mdl$hru, function(h) h$id == hid))
  is_ch <- !is.na(mdl$hru[[idx]]$class$endNode)
  # Find nearest downstream channel if hillslope
  sf_down <- mdl$hru[[idx]]$sf_flow_direction$id
  cat("HRU", hid, "| is channel:", is_ch, 
      "| sf flows to:", sf_down, "\n")
}

# Build a lookup: hillslope id → nearest downstream channel id
hs_to_channel <- function(hid, hru_list){
  idx <- which(sapply(hru_list, function(h) h$id == hid))
  h   <- hru_list[[idx]]
  if(!is.na(h$class$endNode)) return(hid)  # already a channel
  # Follow sf_flow_direction until we hit a channel
  next_id <- h$sf_flow_direction$id
  if(length(next_id) == 0) return(NA_integer_)
  hs_to_channel(next_id[1], hru_list)  # recurse
}

# Remap pa_obs hru_ids to nearest downstream channel
pa_obs$hru_id_orig    <- pa_obs$hru_id
pa_obs$hru_id_channel <- sapply(pa_obs$hru_id, hs_to_channel, hru_list = mdl$hru)

cat("HRU remapping:\n")
print(unique(pa_obs[, c("hru_id_orig", "hru_id_channel")]))

# Apply the remapping
pa_obs$hru_id <- pa_obs$hru_id_channel

## second thing
for(ii in seq_along(mdl$hru)){
  if(!is.na(mdl$hru[[ii]]$class$endNode)){
    cat("HRU", mdl$hru[[ii]]$id, 
        "| sf type:", mdl$hru[[ii]]$sf$type,
        "| parameters:", mdl$hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters, "\n")
  }
}

# Run with very conservative parameters to isolate where the blow-up starts
safe_params <- setNames(param_bounds$lower, param_bounds$name)
safe_params["t_d"]   <- 28800   # 8 hours
safe_params["n_ch"]  <- 0.05
safe_params["t_raf"] <- 900

safe_sim <- run_model(safe_params, mdl, obs_cal, outlet_id = OUTLET_ID)
if(!is.null(safe_sim)){
  cat("Safe run q_out range:", range(as.numeric(safe_sim[,"q_out"])), "\n")
} else {
  cat("Safe run returned NULL\n")
}

#
# Step 1: expose the actual error in serial
test_params <- setNames(param_bounds$lower, param_bounds$name)
test_params["t_d"] <- 7200  # keep above guard

# Temporarily bypass tryCatch in run_model by calling internals directly
hru <- mdl$hru
obs_input <- strip_obs(obs_cal)
total_area <- sum(sapply(hru, function(h) h$properties["area"]))
q_init <- as.numeric(na.omit(obs_cal$Discharge_m3_s))[1]

# Walk through the parameter-setting loop and find where it breaks
for(ii in seq_along(hru)){
  cat("Setting HRU", hru[[ii]]$id, "...\n")
  is_channel <- !is.na(hru[[ii]]$class$endNode)
  cat("  is_channel:", is_channel, "\n")
  
  if(!is_channel){
    hru[[ii]]$sz$parameters["t_0"]     <- test_params["t_0"]
    hru[[ii]]$sz$parameters["m"]       <- test_params["m"]
    hru[[ii]]$uz$parameters["t_d"]     <- test_params["t_d"]
    hru[[ii]]$rz$parameters["s_rzmax"] <- test_params["s_rzmax"]
    if(hru[[ii]]$sf$type == "cnst")
      hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["c_sf"]  <- test_params["c_sf"]
  } else {
    hru[[ii]]$sz$parameters["t_0"]     <- 0.001
    hru[[ii]]$rz$parameters["s_rzmax"] <- 0.001
    if(hru[[ii]]$sf$type == "cnst")
      hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["c_sf"]  <- test_params["v_ch"]
  }
  
  # Snow — this is the most likely failure point
  cat("  snow structure:", names(hru[[ii]]$snow), "\n")
  cat("  snow parameters:", names(hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters), "\n")
  hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters["T_T"]  <- test_params["T_T"]
  hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters["C_f"]  <- test_params["C_f"]
  hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters["C_wh"] <- test_params["C_wh"]
  
  hru[[ii]]$initialisation["s_rz_0"]    <- 0.3
  hru[[ii]]$initialisation["r_uz_sz_0"] <- q_init / total_area
}
cat("Parameter loop completed successfully\n")

# Test a single worker directly with a known-good parameter set
cl <- makeCluster(1L)

clusterExport(cl,
  c("mdl_template", "obs", "obs_q", "pa_obs",
    "param_bounds", "n_params",
    "FLOW_THRESHOLD", "W_KGE", "W_CSI",
    "KGE_THRESHOLD", "CSI_THRESHOLD",
    "outlet_id",
    "run_model", "strip_obs",
    "kge", "csi_presence_absence",
    "glue_likelihood", "glue_eval"),
  envir = environment())
clusterEvalQ(cl, { library(dynatop); library(xts) })

# Run one evaluation on the worker with full error detail
test_vec <- (param_bounds$lower + param_bounds$upper) / 2
test_vec[param_bounds$name == "t_d"] <- 7200

result <- clusterCall(cl, function(pv){
  tryCatch({
    glue_eval(pv)
  }, error = function(e){
    # Return the full error message AND the call stack
    list(error = conditionMessage(e),
         call  = paste(deparse(conditionCall(e)), collapse=" "))
  })
}, pv = test_vec)

stopCluster(cl)
cat("Worker result:\n")
print(result[[1]])

# Stop any existing cluster first
if(exists("cl")) try(stopCluster(cl), silent=TRUE)

cl <- makeCluster(1L)
clusterEvalQ(cl, { library(dynatop); library(xts) })
clusterExport(cl,
  c("mdl_template", "obs", "obs_q", "pa_obs",
    "param_bounds", "n_params",
    "FLOW_THRESHOLD", "W_KGE", "W_CSI",
    "KGE_THRESHOLD", "CSI_THRESHOLD",
    "outlet_id",
    "run_model", "strip_obs",
    "kge", "csi_presence_absence",
    "glue_likelihood", "glue_eval"),
  envir = .GlobalEnv)

# Send one parameter vector and catch everything
test_vec <- (param_bounds$lower + param_bounds$upper) / 2
test_vec[param_bounds$name == "t_d"] <- 7200

result <- parLapply(cl, list(test_vec), function(pv){
  tryCatch(
    withCallingHandlers(
      glue_eval(pv),
      error   = function(e) cat("ERROR:", conditionMessage(e), "\n"),
      warning = function(w) cat("WARN:", conditionMessage(w), "\n")
    ),
    error = function(e){
      # Capture full traceback
      list(
        error = conditionMessage(e),
        trace = paste(capture.output(traceback()), collapse="\n")
      )
    }
  )
})

stopCluster(cl)
print(result[[1]])

required_exports <- c("mdl_template", "obs", "obs_q", "pa_obs",
                       "param_bounds", "n_params",
                       "FLOW_THRESHOLD", "W_KGE", "W_CSI",
                       "KGE_THRESHOLD", "CSI_THRESHOLD",
                       "outlet_id", "run_model", "strip_obs",
                       "kge", "csi_presence_absence",
                       "glue_likelihood", "glue_eval")

for(nm in required_exports){
  exists_global <- exists(nm, envir = .GlobalEnv, inherits = FALSE)
  cat(nm, ":", ifelse(exists_global, "OK", "MISSING"), "\n")
}

cl <- makeCluster(1L)
clusterEvalQ(cl, { library(dynatop); library(xts) })
clusterExport(cl, c("mdl_template", "param_bounds", "run_model", 
                    "strip_obs", "obs_cal", "outlet_id"),
              envir = .GlobalEnv)

result <- parLapply(cl, list(1), function(dummy){
  # Try just dynatop$new() in isolation
  hru <- mdl_template$hru
  tryCatch(
    dynatop$new(hru, map = mdl_template$map),
    error = function(e) list(error = conditionMessage(e))
  )
})
stopCluster(cl)
cat("dynatop$new() on worker:", result[[1]]$error, "\n")

######
cl <- makeCluster(1L)
clusterEvalQ(cl, { library(dynatop); library(xts) })
clusterExport(cl, c("mdl_template", "param_bounds", "strip_obs", 
                    "obs_cal", "outlet_id", "pa_obs",
                    "FLOW_THRESHOLD", "obs_q"),
              envir = .GlobalEnv)

result <- parLapply(cl, list(1), function(dummy){
  hru       <- mdl_template$hru
  obs_input <- strip_obs(obs_cal)
  
  # Step 1
  cm <- tryCatch(dynatop$new(hru, map = mdl_template$map),
                 error = function(e) return(list(failed_at="new", error=conditionMessage(e))))
  if(is.list(cm) && !is.null(cm$failed_at)) return(cm)
  cat("new: OK\n")
  
  # Step 2
  tryCatch(cm$add_data(obs_input),
           error = function(e) return(list(failed_at="add_data", error=conditionMessage(e))))
  cat("add_data: OK\n")
  
  # Step 3
  tryCatch(cm$initialise(),
           error = function(e) return(list(failed_at="initialise", error=conditionMessage(e))))
  cat("initialise: OK\n")
  
  # Step 4
  out_rows <- data.frame(name="q_out", id=outlet_id, flux="q_sf",
                         scale=1.0, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
  tryCatch(cm$sim(out_rows, sub_step=900)$get_output(),
           error = function(e) return(list(failed_at="sim", error=conditionMessage(e))))
})

stopCluster(cl)
print(result[[1]])
######
# Print the actual source of run_model as the worker sees it
cl <- makeCluster(1L)
clusterEvalQ(cl, { library(dynatop); library(xts) })
clusterExport(cl, c("run_model", "strip_obs", "mdl_template"),
              envir = .GlobalEnv)

result <- parLapply(cl, list(1), function(dummy){
  # Find the is_channel line in run_model
  src <- deparse(body(run_model))
  src[grep("is_channel", src)]
})
stopCluster(cl)
print(result[[1]])

# Confirm the global run_model has the fix
src <- deparse(body(run_model))
cat(src[grep("is_channel", src)], sep="\n")

# Temporarily replace glue_eval with a diagnostic version
glue_eval_debug <- function(param_vec){
  params <- setNames(param_vec, param_bounds$name)
  
  cat("Step 1: guards\n")
  if(params["t_d"]    < 900)  return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))
  if(params["s_rzmax"] <= 0)  return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))
  
  cat("Step 2: run_model\n")
  sim <- tryCatch(
    run_model(params, mdl_template, obs, outlet_id,
              pa_hru_ids = unique(pa_obs$hru_id)),
    error   = function(e) list(failed_at="run_model", error=conditionMessage(e)),
    warning = function(w) list(failed_at="run_model_warn", error=conditionMessage(w))
  )
  if(is.list(sim) && !is.null(sim$failed_at)) return(sim)
  cat("run_model: OK, sim is", class(sim), "\n")
  
  if(is.null(sim)) return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))
  
  cat("Step 3: blow-up check\n")
  sim_vals <- as.numeric(sim)
  if(any(!is.finite(sim_vals)) || any(abs(sim_vals) > 1e3))
    return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))
  
  cat("Step 4: kge\n")
  kge_val <- tryCatch(
    kge(sim[, "q_out"], obs_q),
    error = function(e) list(failed_at="kge", error=conditionMessage(e))
  )
  if(is.list(kge_val)) return(kge_val)
  cat("kge:", kge_val, "\n")
  
  cat("Step 5: csi\n")
  pa_scores <- tryCatch(
    csi_presence_absence(sim, pa_obs),
    error = function(e) list(failed_at="csi", error=conditionMessage(e))
  )
  if(is.list(pa_scores) && !is.null(pa_scores$failed_at)) return(pa_scores)
  csi_val <- if(!is.null(pa_scores) && is.finite(pa_scores$CSI)) pa_scores$CSI else 0
  cat("csi:", csi_val, "\n")
  
  cat("Step 6: likelihood\n")
  L <- glue_likelihood(kge_val, csi_val)
  cat("L:", L, "\n")
  
  list(L = L, kge = kge_val, csi = csi_val)
}

# Run debug version on a single worker
cl <- makeCluster(1L)
clusterEvalQ(cl, { library(dynatop); library(xts) })
clusterExport(cl,
  c("mdl_template", "obs", "obs_q", "pa_obs",
    "param_bounds", "n_params",
    "FLOW_THRESHOLD", "W_KGE", "W_CSI",
    "KGE_THRESHOLD", "CSI_THRESHOLD",
    "outlet_id", "run_model", "strip_obs",
    "kge", "csi_presence_absence",
    "glue_likelihood", "glue_eval_debug"),
  envir = .GlobalEnv)

test_vec <- (param_bounds$lower + param_bounds$upper) / 2
test_vec[param_bounds$name == "t_d"] <- 7200

result <- parLapply(cl, list(test_vec), function(pv){
  glue_eval_debug(pv)
})
stopCluster(cl)
print(result[[1]])

cl <- makeCluster(1L)
clusterEvalQ(cl, { library(dynatop); library(xts) })
clusterExport(cl, c("param_bounds", "n_params"), envir = .GlobalEnv)

test_vec <- (param_bounds$lower + param_bounds$upper) / 2
test_vec[param_bounds$name == "t_d"] <- 7200

result <- parLapply(cl, list(test_vec), function(pv){
  params <- setNames(pv, param_bounds$name)
  list(
    params    = params,
    t_d_val   = params["t_d"],
    t_d_check = params["t_d"] < 900,
    s_rz_val  = params["s_rzmax"],
    s_rz_check = params["s_rzmax"] <= 0,
    names_match = all(param_bounds$name %in% names(params))
  )
})
stopCluster(cl)
print(result[[1]])
```

```{r hopefully better param}
# =============================================================================
# dynatop GLUE Calibration — fully corrected version
# Objectives: (1) Discharge KGE via q_sf at outlet HRU 0
#             (2) Flow presence/absence CSI at channel observation points
# =============================================================================

#library(dynatop)


# =============================================================================
# 1. LOAD MODEL AND DATA
# =============================================================================

#mdl <- readRDS(file.path(".", "hb_data", "dynaGIS", "atb_20.rds"))
#obs <- readRDS(file.path(".", "processed", "obs.rds"))
# obs columns: Discharge_ls, Discharge_m3_s, precip (m/step), pet (m/step), temp (°C)
# precip and pet must already be in m/step, not mm

# ── Calibration window ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cal_start <- as.POSIXct("2023-05-15", tz = "UTC")
cal_end   <- as.POSIXct("2024-05-31", tz = "UTC")
obs_cal   <- obs[index(obs) >= cal_start & index(obs) <= cal_end, ]

# ── Observed discharge for KGE — subset to calibration window ─────────────────
obs_q <- obs_cal$Discharge_m3_s

# ── Confirm outlet HRU ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for(ii in seq_along(mdl$hru)){
  h <- mdl$hru[[ii]]
  if(length(h$sf_flow_direction$id) == 0 & length(h$sz_flow_direction$id) == 0)
    cat("Outlet HRU id:", h$id, "| is channel:", !is.na(h$class$endNode), "\n")
}
OUTLET_ID <- 0L  # confirm from output above

# =============================================================================
# 2. FLOW PRESENCE/ABSENCE OBSERVATIONS
# =============================================================================

all_24 <- read_csv("/Users/johnmorgan/Documents/VT Research/HBTopModel/STIC_Synthesis/all_24.csv")

# Snap STIC locations to nearest channel HRU within 30 m
stic_locs <- all_24 %>%
  filter(wshed == "W3") %>%
  select(number, lat, long) %>%
  unique() %>%
  vect(geom = c("long", "lat"), crs = "EPSG:4326") %>%
  project("EPSG:26919")

all_out <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(stic_locs)), function(i){
  dist_table <- distance(stic_locs[i,], result$conv_channel)
  idx        <- which(dist_table == min(dist_table))[1]
  data.frame(ID         = stic_locs[i,]$number,
             channel_ID = result$conv_channel[idx,]$name,
             dist       = min(dist_table))
}))

chosen <- all_out %>% filter(dist <= 30)

pa_obs <- all_24 %>%
  filter(wshed == "W3", number %in% chosen$ID) %>%
  select(datetime, number, wetdry) %>%
  rename(ID = number) %>%
  left_join(chosen, by = "ID", multiple = "first") %>%
  mutate(
    observed = as.integer(case_when(wetdry == "wet" ~ 1, wetdry == "dry" ~ 0)),
    hru_id   = as.integer(channel_ID),
    datetime = as.POSIXct(datetime, tz = "UTC")
  ) %>%
  select(hru_id, datetime, observed) %>%
  filter(datetime >= cal_start, datetime <= cal_end) %>%
  filter(!is.na(hru_id), !is.na(observed))

cat("PA observation sites (HRU ids):", unique(pa_obs$hru_id), "\n")
cat("PA observations:", nrow(pa_obs),
    "| flowing:", sum(pa_obs$observed == 1),
    "| dry:", sum(pa_obs$observed == 0), "\n")

# Flow threshold: simulated q_sf (m³/s) above which channel is "flowing".
# Set to a small fraction of typical baseflow. Adjust based on diagnostic below.
FLOW_THRESHOLD <- 1e-8

# =============================================================================
# 3. PARAMETER SPACE
# =============================================================================

param_bounds <- data.frame(
  name  = c("t_0",     # saturated zone transmissivity at saturation (m²/s)
             "m",       # exponential decay constant (m⁻¹)
             "t_d",     # unsaturated zone time delay (s) — must be >> timestep (900s)
             "s_rzmax", # root zone max storage (m)
             "c_sf",    # hillslope surface velocity (m/s)
             "n_ch",    # (Manning's n for natural channels)
             "v_ch", # velocity of the channel, in mdl c_sf, but in channel HRUs
             "T_T",     # snow/rain temperature threshold (°C)
             "C_f",     # degree-day melt factor (m/°C/day)
             "C_wh"),   # snowfall correction factor (-)
  lower = c(0.001,  0.005,  7200,   0.05,  0.1,  0.02,  0.3,
            0.0,  0.001,  0.5),
  upper = c(5.0,    0.08,   172800, 0.5,   1.0,  0.15,  0.3,
            2.0,  0.008,  1.5),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
n_params <- nrow(param_bounds)

# =============================================================================
# 4. MODEL RUNNER
# =============================================================================
# KEY FIXES vs previous version:
#   - Output flux is q_sf at OUTLET_ID (not v_sf_rz, which is infiltration flux)
#   - Discharge column is stripped from obs inside this function — always consistent
#   - baseflow derived from calibration period start, not full obs start
#   - s_rz_0 starts at 0.3 (drier) to avoid immediate surface runoff spike
#   - Channel c_sf is printed to catch cases where sf type is not "cnst"

# Helper: strip non-forcing columns before passing to dynatop
strip_obs <- function(obs_xts){
  drop <- c("Discharge_m3_s", "Discharge_ls", "Discharge")
  keep <- !names(obs_xts) %in% drop
  obs_xts[, keep]
}

run_model <- function(params,
                      mdl_template,
                      obs,
                      outlet_id  = 0L,
                      pa_hru_ids = unique(pa_obs$hru_id),
                      sub_step   = 900){

  hru        <- mdl_template$hru
  obs_input  <- strip_obs(obs)  # remove discharge — dynatop only wants forcings
  total_area <- sum(sapply(hru, function(h) h$properties["area"]))

  # Baseflow for initialisation: use first valid value in obs window
  q_init   <- as.numeric(na.omit(obs$Discharge_m3_s))[1]
  if(!is.finite(q_init) || q_init <= 0) q_init <- 1e-5

  for(ii in seq_along(hru)){
    #is_channel <- !is.na(hru[[ii]]$class$endNode)
    is_channel <- isTRUE(!is.na(hru[[ii]]$class$endNode))


    if(!is_channel){
      # ── Hillslope ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      hru[[ii]]$sz$parameters["t_0"]     <- params["t_0"]
      hru[[ii]]$sz$parameters["m"]       <- params["m"]
      hru[[ii]]$uz$parameters["t_d"]     <- params["t_d"]
      hru[[ii]]$rz$parameters["s_rzmax"] <- params["s_rzmax"]

      if(hru[[ii]]$sf$type == "cnst"){
        hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["c_sf"]  <- params["c_sf"]
      }
      # kin type: leave Manning n at its dynatopGIS-derived value
      # (add "n" to param_bounds if you want to calibrate it)

    } else {
      # ── Channel ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      # Keep channel subsurface transmissivity very low — channels don't
      # have meaningful lateral subsurface flow in this implementation
      hru[[ii]]$sz$parameters["t_0"]     <- 0.001
      hru[[ii]]$rz$parameters["s_rzmax"] <- 0.001

      if(hru[[ii]]$sf$type == "cnst"){
        hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["c_sf"]  <- params["v_ch"]
      } else if(hru[[ii]]$sf$type == "kin"){
        # For kinematic wave channels, velocity is derived from Manning n
        # and slope — leave as-is, or add n_ch to param_bounds
        hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["n"]     <- params["n_ch"]  # Manning's n
        hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["t_raf"] <- 0               # no detention
        hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters["s_raf"] <- 0
      }
    }

    # ── Snow parameters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # Adapt these names to match your C++ implementation's parameter names
    hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters["T_T"]  <- params["T_T"]
    hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters["C_f"]  <- params["C_f"]
    hru[[ii]]$snow$parameters["C_wh"] <- params["C_wh"]

    # ── Initial conditions ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # s_rz_0 = 0.3: start relatively dry to avoid an initial surface runoff
    # spike. Raise toward 0.6-0.8 for wet season starts.
    hru[[ii]]$initialisation["s_rz_0"]    <- 0.3
    hru[[ii]]$initialisation["r_uz_sz_0"] <- q_init / total_area
  }

  # ── Output definition ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # q_sf at the outlet is the catchment discharge in m³/s.
  # v_sf_rz is infiltration flux INTO the root zone — NOT discharge.
  pa_ids  <- unique(pa_hru_ids)
  out_rows <- rbind(
    data.frame(name  = "q_out",
               id    = outlet_id,
               flux  = "q_sf",    # ← CORRECT: surface outflow at outlet
               scale = 1.0,
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
    data.frame(name  = paste0("q_sf_hru", pa_ids),
               id    = pa_ids,
               flux  = "q_sf",
               scale = 1.0,
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  )

  result <- tryCatch({
    cm <- dynatop$new(hru, map = mdl_template$map)
    cm$add_data(obs_input)
    cm$initialise()
    cm$sim(out_rows, sub_step = sub_step)$get_output()
  }, error = function(e){
    NULL
  })

  return(result)
}

# =============================================================================
# 5. DIAGNOSTIC — run before calibration to confirm model is working
# =============================================================================
# Checks:
#   (a) q_sf at outlet tracks observed discharge with correct order of magnitude
#   (b) s_sf at outlet does NOT grow unboundedly through time
#   (c) Simulated q_sf at PA sites is non-zero during observed flowing periods

run_diagnostic <- function(mdl, obs_cal, pa_obs,
                           outlet_id = 0L, sub_step = 900){

  obs_input <- strip_obs(obs_cal)

  diag_rows <- data.frame(
    name  = c("q_sf_out", "s_sf_out", "s_sz_out", "q_sz_out"),
    id    = c(outlet_id, outlet_id, outlet_id, outlet_id),
    flux  = c("q_sf", "s_sf", "s_sz", "q_sz"),
    scale = c(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  cm <- dynatop$new(mdl$hru, map = mdl$map)
  cm$add_data(obs_input)
  cm$initialise()
  diag_out <- cm$sim(diag_rows, sub_step = sub_step)$get_output()

  cat("=== Outlet diagnostics ===\n")
  cat("q_sf range (m³/s):", range(as.numeric(diag_out[,"q_sf_out"])), "\n")
  cat("s_sf range (m³):  ", range(as.numeric(diag_out[,"s_sf_out"])), "\n")
  cat("q_sz range (m³/s):", range(as.numeric(diag_out[,"q_sz_out"])), "\n")
  cat("obs_q range (m³/s):", range(as.numeric(obs_q), na.rm=TRUE), "\n")

  # s_sf should NOT grow monotonically — check slope
  s_sf_vals <- as.numeric(diag_out[,"s_sf_out"])
  if(s_sf_vals[length(s_sf_vals)] > 10 * s_sf_vals[1] + 1){
    cat("WARNING: s_sf is growing unboundedly — channel surface is not draining.\n")
    cat("  Check that channel HRU sf type is 'cnst' and v_ch > 0.\n")
    cat("  Channel HRU sf types:\n")
    for(ii in seq_along(mdl$hru)){
      if(!is.na(mdl$hru[[ii]]$class$endNode))
        cat("    HRU", mdl$hru[[ii]]$id, "sf type:", mdl$hru[[ii]]$sf$type,
            "| c_sf:", mdl$hru[[ii]]$sf$parameters, "\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat("s_sf appears stable — channel is draining correctly.\n")
  }

  # PA site check
  pa_rows <- data.frame(
```
