```{r hopefully better param}
    name  = paste0("q_sf_hru", unique(pa_obs$hru_id)),
    id    = unique(pa_obs$hru_id),
    flux  = "q_sf",
    scale = 1.0,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  cm2 <- dynatop$new(mdl$hru, map = mdl$map)
  cm2$add_data(obs_input)
  cm2$initialise()
  pa_out <- cm2$sim(pa_rows, sub_step = sub_step)$get_output()

  cat("\n=== PA site q_sf ranges ===\n")
  for(hid in unique(pa_obs$hru_id)){
    col <- paste0("q_sf_hru", hid)
    flowing_times <- pa_obs$datetime[pa_obs$hru_id == hid &
                                       pa_obs$observed == 1]
    if(col %in% names(pa_out) && length(flowing_times) > 0){
      sim_during_flow <- as.numeric(pa_out[flowing_times, col])
      cat(sprintf("HRU %d | during observed flow: min=%.2e max=%.2e | threshold=%.2e\n",
                  hid, min(sim_during_flow, na.rm=TRUE),
                  max(sim_during_flow, na.rm=TRUE), FLOW_THRESHOLD))
    }
  }

  invisible(list(diag_out = diag_out, pa_out = pa_out))
}

cat("Running diagnostic with default parameters...\n")
diag <- run_diagnostic(mdl, obs_cal, pa_obs, outlet_id = OUTLET_ID)

# =============================================================================
# 6. OBJECTIVE FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

kge <- function(sim, obs){
  both  <- merge(sim, obs, join = "inner")
  s     <- as.numeric(both[, 1])
  o     <- as.numeric(both[, 2])
  valid <- is.finite(s) & is.finite(o) & abs(s) < 1e3
  if(sum(valid) < 10) return(-Inf)
  s <- s[valid]; o <- o[valid]
  r <- cor(s, o)
  if(!is.finite(r)) return(-Inf)
  a <- sd(s) / sd(o)
  b <- mean(s) / mean(o)
  1 - sqrt((r - 1)^2 + (a - 1)^2 + (b - 1)^2)
}

csi_presence_absence <- function(sim_output, pa_obs,
                                  threshold = FLOW_THRESHOLD){
  TP <- 0L; FP <- 0L; FN <- 0L; TN <- 0L

  for(hid in unique(pa_obs$hru_id)){
    col_name <- paste0("q_sf_hru", hid)
    if(!col_name %in% names(sim_output)) next

    sub_obs <- pa_obs[pa_obs$hru_id == hid, ]
    if(nrow(sub_obs) == 0) next

    sub_xts <- xts(sub_obs$observed,
                   order.by = as.POSIXct(sub_obs$datetime, tz = "UTC"))
    both    <- merge(sim_output[, col_name], sub_xts, join = "inner")
    if(nrow(both) == 0) next

    sim_bin <- as.integer(as.numeric(both[, 1]) >= threshold)
    obs_bin <- as.integer(as.numeric(both[, 2]))
    valid   <- !is.na(sim_bin) & !is.na(obs_bin)
    sim_bin <- sim_bin[valid]; obs_bin <- obs_bin[valid]
    if(length(sim_bin) == 0) next

    TP <- TP + sum(sim_bin == 1L & obs_bin == 1L)
    FP <- FP + sum(sim_bin == 1L & obs_bin == 0L)
    FN <- FN + sum(sim_bin == 0L & obs_bin == 1L)
    TN <- TN + sum(sim_bin == 0L & obs_bin == 0L)
  }

  denom <- TP + FP + FN
  if(denom == 0) return(NULL)

  list(
    CSI      = TP / denom,
    POD      = if((TP + FN) > 0) TP / (TP + FN) else 0,  # recall/sensitivity
    FAR      = if((TP + FP) > 0) FP / (TP + FP) else 0,  # false alarm rate
    F1       = if((2*TP + FP + FN) > 0) 2*TP / (2*TP + FP + FN) else 0,
    accuracy = if((TP+FP+FN+TN) > 0) (TP+TN)/(TP+FP+FN+TN) else 0,
    TP = TP, FP = FP, FN = FN, TN = TN
  )
}

# =============================================================================
# 7. GLUE
# =============================================================================

# Weights — must sum to 1
W_KGE <- 0.6
W_CSI <- 0.4

# Behavioural thresholds — start permissive, tighten once runs are working
KGE_THRESHOLD <- -0.5  # raise toward 0.3-0.5 once model is producing good runs
CSI_THRESHOLD <- 0.0

glue_likelihood <- function(kge_val, csi_val){
  if(!is.finite(kge_val) || kge_val < KGE_THRESHOLD) return(0)
  if(!is.finite(csi_val) || csi_val < CSI_THRESHOLD) return(0)

  kge_scaled <- (kge_val - KGE_THRESHOLD) / (1 - KGE_THRESHOLD)
  csi_scaled <- csi_val  # already 0-1

  # Avoid 0^0 = 1 when weight is 0
  L_kge <- if(W_KGE > 0) kge_scaled ^ W_KGE else 1
  L_csi <- if(W_CSI > 0) csi_scaled ^ W_CSI else 1

  L_kge * L_csi
}

glue_eval <- function(param_vec){
    params <- setNames(param_vec, param_bounds$name)

    # Hard guards — reject physically implausible combinations immediately
    if(params["t_d"]    < 900)    return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))
    if(params["v_ch"]   <= 0)     return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))
    if(params["s_rzmax"] <= 0)    return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))

    sim <- run_model(params, mdl_template, obs, outlet_id,
                     pa_hru_ids = unique(pa_obs$hru_id))

    if(is.null(sim)) return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))

    sim_vals <- as.numeric(sim)
    if(any(!is.finite(sim_vals)) || any(abs(sim_vals) > 1e3)){
      return(list(L=0, kge=NA, csi=NA))
    }

    kge_val   <- kge(sim[, "q_out"], obs_q)
    pa_scores <- csi_presence_absence(sim, pa_obs)
    csi_val   <- if(!is.null(pa_scores) && is.finite(pa_scores$CSI)){
      pa_scores$CSI
    } else { 0 }

    L <- glue_likelihood(kge_val, csi_val)
    list(L = L, kge = kge_val, csi = csi_val)
  }

run_glue <- function(n_samples,
                     mdl_template,
                     obs,
                     pa_obs,
                     outlet_id = 0L,
                     n_cores   = 1L,
                     seed      = 42L){
  set.seed(seed)
  lhs_design   <- randomLHS(n_samples, n_params)
  param_matrix <- sweep(lhs_design, 2,
                        param_bounds$upper - param_bounds$lower, "*")
  param_matrix <- sweep(param_matrix, 2, param_bounds$lower, "+")
  colnames(param_matrix) <- param_bounds$name

  cat(sprintf("Running %d GLUE samples on %d core(s)...\n",
              n_samples, n_cores))

  

  if(n_cores > 1L){
    cl <- makeCluster(n_cores)
    on.exit(stopCluster(cl), add = TRUE)

    clusterExport(cl,
      c("mdl_template", 
        "obs", "obs_q", "pa_obs",
        "param_bounds", "n_params",
        "FLOW_THRESHOLD", "W_KGE", "W_CSI",
        "KGE_THRESHOLD", "CSI_THRESHOLD",
        "outlet_id",
        "run_model", "strip_obs",
        "kge", "csi_presence_absence",
        "glue_likelihood", "glue_eval"),
      envir = environment())
    clusterEvalQ(cl, { library(dynatop); library(xts) })

    score_list <- parApply(cl, param_matrix, 1, glue_eval)
  } else {
    score_list <- apply(param_matrix, 1, glue_eval)
  }

  results <- data.frame(
    param_matrix,
    L   = sapply(score_list, `[[`, "L"),
    kge = sapply(score_list, `[[`, "kge"),
    csi = sapply(score_list, `[[`, "csi")
  )

  n_behav <- sum(results$L > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("Behavioural runs: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
              n_behav, n_samples, 100 * n_behav / n_samples))

  if(n_behav == 0)
    cat("No behavioural runs — lower KGE_THRESHOLD or run diagnostic.\n")

  results$L_norm <- ifelse(sum(results$L) > 0,
                           results$L / sum(results$L), NA_real_)

  results[order(-results$L), ]
}

# =============================================================================
# 8. UNCERTAINTY BOUNDS
# =============================================================================

glue_uncertainty_bounds <- function(glue_results, mdl_template, obs, obs_q,
                                     outlet_id = 0L,
                                     quantiles  = c(0.05, 0.95)){
  behav <- glue_results[glue_results$L > 0 & !is.na(glue_results$L), ]
  if(nrow(behav) == 0) stop("No behavioural runs.")
  cat(sprintf("Computing uncertainty bounds from %d behavioural runs...\n",
              nrow(behav)))

  sim_list <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(behav)), function(i){
    params <- setNames(as.numeric(behav[i, param_bounds$name]), param_bounds$name)
    sim    <- run_model(params, mdl_template, obs, outlet_id)
    if(is.null(sim)) return(NULL)
    as.numeric(sim[, "q_out"])
  })

  good      <- !sapply(sim_list, is.null)
  sim_list  <- sim_list[good]
  L_w       <- behav$L_norm[good]
  L_w       <- L_w / sum(L_w)  # re-normalise over successful runs
  sim_matrix <- do.call(cbind, sim_list)

  bounds <- data.frame(
    datetime = index(strip_obs(obs)),
    q_obs    = as.numeric(obs_q),
    q_best   = sim_matrix[, which.max(L_w)],
    q_mean   = apply(sim_matrix, 1, weighted.mean, w = L_w)
  )

  for(q in quantiles){
    col_name <- paste0("q_", sprintf("%02d", round(q * 100)))
    bounds[[col_name]] <- apply(sim_matrix, 1, function(row){
      ord  <- order(row)
      cumw <- cumsum(L_w[ord])
      row[ord][which(cumw >= q)[1]]
    })
  }
  bounds
}

plot_glue_bounds <- function(bounds){
  t_idx  <- bounds$datetime
  y_max  <- max(c(bounds$q_obs, bounds$q_95), na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1

  plot(t_idx, bounds$q_obs, type = "l", col = "black", lwd = 1.5,
       ylim = c(0, y_max),
       main = "GLUE uncertainty bounds vs observed [m³/s]",
       xlab = "", ylab = "m³/s")

  polygon(c(t_idx, rev(t_idx)),
          c(bounds$q_05, rev(bounds$q_95)),
          col = adjustcolor("steelblue", alpha.f = 0.3), border = NA)

  lines(t_idx, bounds$q_best, col = "firebrick", lwd = 1.5)
  lines(t_idx, bounds$q_mean, col = "steelblue", lwd = 1, lty = 2)

  legend("topright",
         legend = c("Observed", "Best likelihood", "Weighted mean", "5–95% band"),
         col    = c("black", "firebrick", "steelblue",
                    adjustcolor("steelblue", 0.4)),
         lwd    = c(1.5, 1.5, 1, 8), lty = c(1, 1, 2, 1))
}

plot_glue_dotty <- function(glue_results){
  behav    <- glue_results[glue_results$L > 0 & !is.na(glue_results$L), ]
  nonbehav <- glue_results[is.na(glue_results$L) | glue_results$L == 0, ]

  par(mfrow = c(3, 3))
  for(pn in param_bounds$name){
    ymax <- if(nrow(behav) > 0) max(behav$L, na.rm=TRUE) else 1
    xlim <- c(param_bounds$lower[param_bounds$name == pn],
              param_bounds$upper[param_bounds$name == pn])
    plot(nonbehav[[pn]], nonbehav$L,
         xlab = pn, ylab = "Likelihood",
         pch = 16, cex = 0.4, col = "grey70",
         ylim = c(0, ymax), xlim = xlim)
    if(nrow(behav) > 0)
      points(behav[[pn]], behav$L, pch = 16, cex = 0.7, col = "firebrick")
    abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "grey50")
  }
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}

# =============================================================================
# 9. RUN
# =============================================================================

# ── Quick sanity check: do two extreme param sets give different output? ───────
cat("\nSanity check: comparing extreme parameter sets...\n")
p_low  <- setNames(param_bounds$lower, param_bounds$name)
p_high <- setNames(param_bounds$upper, param_bounds$name)
p_low["t_d"]  <- 7200; p_high["t_d"] <- 86400  # keep above 900s guard

s_low  <- run_model(p_low,  mdl, obs_cal, outlet_id = OUTLET_ID)
s_high <- run_model(p_high, mdl, obs_cal, outlet_id = OUTLET_ID)

if(!is.null(s_low) && !is.null(s_high)){
  cat("low  params q_out range:", range(as.numeric(s_low[,"q_out"])),  "\n")
  cat("high params q_out range:", range(as.numeric(s_high[,"q_out"])), "\n")
  cat("KGE low: ", kge(s_low[,"q_out"],  obs_q), "\n")
  cat("KGE high:", kge(s_high[,"q_out"], obs_q), "\n")
} else {
  cat("One or both extreme runs returned NULL — check run_model errors.\n")
}

mdl_template <<- mdl
outlet_id    <<- OUTLET_ID
hru <- mdl$hru
# ── GLUE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
glue_results <- run_glue(
  n_samples    = 2000,
  mdl_template = mdl,
  obs          = obs_cal,   # discharge stripped inside run_model
  pa_obs       = pa_obs,
  outlet_id    = OUTLET_ID,
  n_cores      = 4L,
  seed         = 42L
)
saveRDS(glue_results, "glue_results.rds")

cat("\nTop 10 behavioural runs:\n")
print(head(glue_results[glue_results$L > 0 & !is.na(glue_results$L),
           c(param_bounds$name, "L", "kge", "csi")], 10))

# =============================================================================
# 10. EVALUATE BEST PARAMETER SET
# =============================================================================

best_params <- setNames(as.numeric(glue_results[1, param_bounds$name]),
                        param_bounds$name)

best_sim <- run_model(best_params, mdl, obs_cal,
                      outlet_id  = OUTLET_ID,
                      pa_hru_ids = unique(pa_obs$hru_id))

cat("\nBest run evaluation:\n")
cat("KGE:", kge(best_sim[,"q_out"], obs_q), "\n")
pa_best <- csi_presence_absence(best_sim, pa_obs)
cat(sprintf("CSI=%.3f  POD=%.3f  FAR=%.3f  TP=%d FP=%d FN=%d TN=%d\n",
            pa_best$CSI, pa_best$POD, pa_best$FAR,
            pa_best$TP, pa_best$FP, pa_best$FN, pa_best$TN))

# ── Discharge plot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot(merge(obs_q, best_sim[,"q_out"]),
     main = "Observed vs Simulated discharge [m³/s]",
     legend.loc = "topright",
     col = c("black", "firebrick"))

# ── Dotty plots ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot_glue_dotty(glue_results)

# ── PA site plots ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for(hid in unique(pa_obs$hru_id)){
  col_name <- paste0("q_sf_hru", hid)
  if(!col_name %in% names(best_sim)) next

  sim_q <- best_sim[, col_name]
  sub   <- pa_obs[pa_obs$hru_id == hid, ]

  plot(sim_q, main = paste("HRU", hid, "— simulated q_sf vs observations"),
       ylab = "m³/s", col = "steelblue")
  abline(h = FLOW_THRESHOLD, lty = 2, col = "grey40")

  flowing <- sub[sub$observed == 1, ]
  dry     <- sub[sub$observed == 0, ]

  if(nrow(flowing) > 0){
    times_f <- sort(unique(as.POSIXct(flowing$datetime, tz="UTC")))
    rect(xleft=head(times_f,-1), xright=tail(times_f,-1),
         ybottom=0, ytop=par("usr")[4],
         col=adjustcolor("green",0.15), border=NA)
  }
  if(nrow(dry) > 0){
    times_d <- sort(unique(as.POSIXct(dry$datetime, tz="UTC")))
    rect(xleft=head(times_d,-1), xright=tail(times_d,-1),
         ybottom=0, ytop=par("usr")[4],
         col=adjustcolor("red",0.1), border=NA)
  }
  legend("topright",
         legend = c("Simulated q_sf", "Flow threshold",
                    "Obs: flowing", "Obs: dry"),
         col    = c("steelblue","grey40",
                    adjustcolor("green",0.4), adjustcolor("red",0.3)),
         lty = c(1,2,NA,NA), pch = c(NA,NA,15,15))
}

# ── Uncertainty bounds (only if enough behavioural runs) ─────────────────────
n_behav <- sum(glue_results$L > 0, na.rm=TRUE)
if(n_behav >= 10){
  bounds <- glue_uncertainty_bounds(glue_results, mdl, obs_cal, obs_q,
                                    outlet_id = OUTLET_ID)
  plot_glue_bounds(bounds)
} else {
  cat("Fewer than 10 behavioural runs — skipping uncertainty bounds.\n",
      "Lower KGE_THRESHOLD or increase n_samples.\n")
}
```



# Out of place code
```{r}

## create a vector of the uid's of the HRUs
hid <- sapply(mdl$hru,function(h){h$id})
## create a vector of the channel names with as storaged in the class variabel
cname <- sapply(mdl$hru,function(h){h$class$name})
idx <- match(gauges$chn_identi, cname) ## index of HRU's corresponding to gauges
## create as a data.frame
gauge_output <- data.frame(name = gauges$Site.Name,
                           id = as.integer(hid[idx]),
                           flux = "q_sf",
                           scale = 1)
saveRDS(gauge_output,file.path(".","dyna","gauge_output_defn.rds")) ## save the output for later use
gauge_output
```
```{r}
# Check if parameters are actually changing between runs
params_low  <- setNames(param_bounds$lower, param_bounds$name)
params_high <- setNames(param_bounds$upper, param_bounds$name)

sim_low  <- run_model(params_low,  mdl, obs_cal[,-1], outlet_id = OUTLET_ID,
                      pa_hru_ids = unique(pa_obs$hru_id))
sim_high <- run_model(params_high, mdl, obs_cal[,-1], outlet_id = OUTLET_ID,
                      pa_hru_ids = unique(pa_obs$hru_id))

cat("sim_low  q_out range:", range(as.numeric(sim_low[,"q_out"])),  "\n")
cat("sim_high q_out range:", range(as.numeric(sim_high[,"q_out"])), "\n")

# Also verify parameters are being set correctly inside run_model
# Add this temporarily at the top of run_model(), after the parameter loop:
cat("t_0 set to:", hru[[1]]$sz$parameters["t_0"], "\n")
cat("m   set to:", hru[[1]]$sz$parameters["m"],   "\n")
```

# Potentially obsolete

```{r}
# RUN MODEL
mdl <- readRDS(file.path(".","dynaGIS","atb_20.rds"))
obs <- readRDS(file.path(".","processed","obs.rds"))
hru <- mdl$hru

#hru <- lapply(hru, function(h){ h$uz$parameters["t_d"] <- 8 * 60 * 60; h})
# 1. Use the outlet_id you generated in your GIS prep chunk
output_defn <- data.frame(name = "W3",
                          id = 1, # <-- USE YOUR VARIABLE HERE
                          flux = "q_sf",
                          scale = 1)


r0 <- as.numeric(obs$Discharge_m3_s[1])
hru <- lapply(hru, function(h) { h$initialisation["r_uz_sz_0"] <- r0; h })

# 3. Run the model
ctch_mdl <- dynatop$new(mdl$hru, map = mdl$map)$
  add_data(obs)$
  initialise()$
  sim(output_defn,
      sub_step = 900)

# 4. Extract and scale flow
sim_rate <- ctch_mdl$get_output() 
sim_vol_m3_day <- sim_rate * 86400 

catchment_area <- sum(sapply(hru, function(x) x$properties["area"]))
sim_specific_m_day <- sim_vol_m3_day / catchment_area

plot(ctch_mdl$get_output(), type = 'p')
# 5. Plot
plot(merge(obs$Discharge_m3_s, sim_rate), 
     main="Simulated vs Observed Specific Discharge (m/day)",
     legend.loc='topleft')
```

```{r map}
# 1. Load the model object
mdl <- readRDS(file.path(".", "dynaGIS", "atb_20.rds"))

# 2. Read the spatial map of HRUs 
# (mdl$map stores the file path to the HRU tif generated by dynatopGIS)
hru_map <- rast(".", "dynaGIS", "atb_20.rds") 

# Rename the raster layer so we can call it easily in ggplot
names(hru_map) <- "hru_id"

# 3. Convert the raster to polygons to calculate the center of each HRU
hru_poly <- as.polygons(hru_map)

# Calculate the centroids (center points) of each polygon for the text labels
hru_centroids <- centroids(hru_poly)

# Extract coordinates into a standard dataframe for ggplot
centroid_coords <- crds(hru_centroids, df = TRUE)
centroid_coords$hru_id <- hru_centroids$hru_id

# 4. Plot using tidyterra and ggplot2
ggplot() +
  # Plot the raster base map (using factor for discrete colors)
  geom_spatraster(data = as.factor(hru_map)) +
  # Overlay the polygon boundaries
  geom_spatvector(data = hru_poly, fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  # Add the ID labels at the center of each HRU
  geom_text(data = centroid_coords, aes(x = x, y = y, label = hru_id), 
            color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  # Use a nice color palette and remove the bulky legend
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", na.value = "transparent", guide = "none") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Hubbard Brook HRU & Channel ID Map",
       subtitle = "Hillslopes (1-20) and Channels (21+)",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude")
```

```{r Mass Balance}
mb <- ctch_mdl$get_mass_errors()
head(mb)
tail(mb)
plot( mb$outflow, main="Mass balance error",ylab="m^3")
plot( mb$e_t, main="Mass balance error",ylab="m^3")

plot( mb$p/catchment_area , main="Mass balance error",ylab="m^3")

plot( mb$initial_state , main="Mass balance error",ylab="m^3")

mdl$output_flux

ctch_mdl$plot_output()
```

```{r}
## extract the current states as a data.frame
current_state <- ctch_mdl$get_states()
## rows correspond to the Hill-slope HRU ID, and the storage's in each of the four zones
head(current_state)

ctch_mdl$plot_state("s_sf")
ctch_mdl$plot_state("s_rz")
ctch_mdl$plot_state("s_uz")
ctch_mdl$plot_state("s_sz")
```

```{r changing parameters}

chn_id <- do.call(c, lapply( hru, function(h){ if(!is.na(h$class$startNode)){ h$id } }))

out_hyd <-  data.frame( name = paste0("hyd_",chn_id),
                       id = chn_id,
                       flux = "v_sf_rz", scale =-1/900) # scale is negative since flux usually downwards, 1/900 is 1/timestep

out_hyd <-  data.frame( name = paste0("hyd_",chn_id),
                       id = chn_id,
                       flux = "v_sf_rz", scale =-1/900)
output_defn <- out_hyd[2,]
## function to compute the relevent part so the output definition for each potential source HRU
fout <- function(h){
    ## test if it is a channel HRU - in which case return NULL
    if( !is.na(h$class$startNode) ){ return(NULL) }
        
    ## index of flow directiuons going to other channels
    idx <- h$sf_flow_direction$id %in% chn_id
    if(!any(idx)){ return(NULL) } ## quit if no links!
    ## create partial output defn
    data.frame(
        name = paste0("hyd_",h$sf_flow_direction$id[idx]), ## which inflow does it alter
        id = h$id, ## flux comes from this HRU
        flux = "q_sf", # surface flux out of this HRU is input to the next
        scale = h$sf_flow_direction$fraction[idx] # scale is fraction of flow (since taking it away)
    )
}
## row bind to give a data.frame
tmp <- do.call( rbind, lapply( hru, fout) )

## row bind onto the intial data frame
out_hyd <- rbind( out_hyd, tmp )

## sort this, not needed but easier to look at
out_hyd <- out_hyd[ order(out_hyd$name), ]

ctch_mdl <- dynatop$new(hru)$add_data(obs)$initialise()$sim(output_defn)
plot( merge(obs$Discharge_m3_s ,ctch_mdl$get_output()), legend.loc='topright')
```
